On January 1, 2025, a Tesla Cybertruck caught fire outside the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas. One person inside the vehicle died, and seven nearby individuals sustained minor injuries. The Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department is investigating the incident, which was first reported at 8:41 a.m. PT.

A bullish put option sweep for TSLA was identified with an expiration date of January 3, 2025. The trade involved 52 contracts at a $400.00 strike price, totaling $51.5K. Prior to this activity, there were 14,271 open contracts at this strike, with 48,970 contracts traded during the session.

Tesla (TSLA) was the most-searched ticker on Benzinga Pro for December 2024. As of December 31, 2024, the stock was priced at $403.84, with a 52-week range of $138.80 to $488.54 and a year-to-date return of +62.6%.

Authorities identified the explosion of a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump International Hotel on January 1, 2025, as a likely suicide. The driver, preliminarily identified as 37-year-old Matthew Livelsberger, died of a self-inflicted gunshot wound before the vehicle, which was loaded with fireworks and gas canisters, detonated. The FBI is investigating the incident.

Tesla reported 495,570 deliveries for Q4 2024, missing the consensus estimate of 506,763. Full-year 2024 deliveries reached 1.79 million, marking the first year-over-year decline from 2023's 1.81 million. Analysts remain divided: Wedbush's Daniel Ives maintains an Outperform rating with a $515 price target, while Ross Gerber criticized the results. Tesla shares fell 6% to $379.28 following the report, with the next earnings update scheduled for January 29.

Tesla shares fell 7% to $377.36 following fourth-quarter production and delivery figures. The company produced 459,445 vehicles and delivered 495,570 vehicles in Q4, compared to 494,989 produced and 484,507 delivered in Q4 2023.

Truist analyst William Stein maintained a Hold rating on Tesla with a $360 price target after a fourth test drive of FSD v13. While the 15-mile test required no interventions, Stein noted imperfections, including a moving violation and failure to yield to a pedestrian, concluding the technology is not ready for prime time.

Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla, ended 2024 with a wealth of $432 billion, a gain of $203 billion, making him the world's richest person. The top 10 richest people globally gained a combined $518 billion in 2024, with Musk being the biggest gainer due to rising Tesla stock prices and SpaceX valuation.

Options activity for Tesla (TSLA) showed a bearish put option sweep expiring January 3, 2025, at a $380 strike price. The trade involved 116 contracts with a total cost of $81.3K, split across 6 trades.

Tesla shares fell following Q4 production of 459,445 vehicles and deliveries of 495,570. Total 2024 deliveries were 1.79 million, the first annual decline from 1.81 million in 2023. The company will report Q4 earnings on Jan. 29, with analysts expecting 74 cents per share and $27.385 billion in revenue.

Tesla shares rose 1.44% in pre-market trading after the Cybertruck qualified for a $7,500 EV tax credit. The credit applies to dual and single-motor variants, bringing effective starting prices to $72,490 and $53,490, respectively, while the $99,990 Cyberbeast does not qualify. Elon Musk also addressed a recent Cybertruck fire in Las Vegas, attributing it to external causes rather than vehicle malfunction.

Tesla plans to deploy its first V4 Superchargers in China by 2025. These V4 cabinets, announced in November 2024, support 400V-1000V architectures, deliver up to 500kW for cars and 1.2 MW for the Semi truck, and can power 8 posts. The Semi is set for production in 2026. Tesla currently has over 60,000 Superchargers globally, with over 11,500 in mainland China.

U.S. stock futures rose on the first trading day of 2025 following a year-end decline. In 2024, the S&P 500 gained 24.01%, the Dow Jones rose 12.80%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 30.78%. Tesla shares were up 1.32% as Elon Musk clarified that a Cybertruck explosion in Las Vegas was unrelated to the vehicle itself. Economic data included a 4.2% year-over-year increase in the S&P CoreLogic Case-Shiller home price index for October.

Tesla opened over 11,000 Supercharger stalls in 2024, bringing the global total to over 65,000. The company added chargers in three new countries during the year. CEO Elon Musk stated that Tesla plans to invest $500 million in network expansion this year, despite previous workforce layoffs in April that impacted 500 members of the charging team.

The Tesla Cybertruck all-wheel drive and single-motor variants now qualify for a $7,500 EV tax credit, reducing effective starting prices to $72,490 and $53,490, respectively. The Cyberbeast variant does not qualify due to its $99,990 price tag. While Tesla representatives reportedly support ending EV subsidies, analysts like Gary Black warn that removing the credit could negatively impact Tesla's EPS.

BYD sold 509,440 new energy passenger vehicles in December, a 50% year-over-year increase. Battery electric vehicle (BEV) sales reached 207,734, while plug-in hybrids reached 301,706. BYD delivered 595,413 BEVs in Q4 2024, likely surpassing Tesla, which is expected to report 501,000 deliveries for the quarter. Tesla's full-year 2023 deliveries totaled 1,808,581.

A deadly attack in New Orleans on New Year's Eve involved a pickup truck and resulted in 15 deaths and 30 injuries. The FBI is investigating the incident as potential terrorism. Separately, Elon Musk commented on a distinct incident involving an explosion in a rented Cybertruck at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, attributing it to fireworks or a bomb in the truck's bed.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk expressed optimism for 2025 with the message 'HNY LFG 2025!!!'. In 2024, Tesla's stock increased by 62.56%. The consensus price target for Tesla is $280.41 based on 33 analysts, with Mizuho setting a high of $515 on December 17.

President Joe Biden announced that federal agencies are investigating a New Year's Day attack in New Orleans that killed 10 people. The FBI is also probing whether an explosion involving a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump Hotel in Las Vegas is connected to the New Orleans incident, though no link has been reported yet.

Elon Musk confirmed that a Cybertruck explosion outside the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas was caused by fireworks or a bomb in the vehicle's bed, not a design flaw. Las Vegas police reported one death and seven injuries, noting the vehicle was rented in Colorado. Authorities are investigating potential links to a separate vehicle attack in New Orleans, as both vehicles were rented from the same company.
Tesla Inc. (TSLA) stock faced pressure after reporting 495,000 deliveries against a 504,000 consensus. In early trade, money flows for TSLA were reported as neutral.

Tesla Inc. (TSLA) experienced its worst weekly performance since late October after reporting 2024 deliveries that were 1% lower than the previous year, marking the company's first year-over-year decline. Analysts consider 2025 a pivotal year for the company to address demand and growth concerns.

XPeng is experiencing a potential recovery signaled by a 'falling wedge' technical pattern. Despite a 17% decline over the past 52 weeks, the company is expanding its global footprint to over 60 markets in 2025 and plans to hire over 6,000 workers this year. November deliveries reached 30,895 vehicles, a 54% year-over-year increase. Traders are considering bull call spreads for January 31, 2025, expiration, targeting price levels of $12 or $13.

Global EV sales are projected to reach 15.1 million units in 2025, a 30% increase from 2024, with China accounting for 29.7% of the market. Tesla's 2024 China sales grew 8.8% to 657,000 vehicles. Competition is intensifying as Tesla, Nio, Li Auto, and BYD utilize discounts and subsidies. XPeng plans to hire over 6,000 employees in 2025 and expand into 60 international markets. Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives maintains an 'Outperform' rating on Tesla with a $515 price target, forecasting 20%-30% delivery growth in 2025.

Tesla stock gained 62.5% in 2024, its fourth-best annual performance since its 2010 IPO, helping the company rejoin the $1 trillion market cap club. The stock hit an all-time high of $488.54 on December 18, 2024. However, Tesla reported its first annual sales decline for 2024, and Q4 deliveries missed Street consensus estimates. Investors are awaiting the Q4 earnings report on January 29, 2025, for guidance on 2025 production and delivery targets.

Tesla stock rose 4.4% on Friday, partially recovering from a 6% decline on Thursday following disappointing Q4 delivery results. Despite the rebound, the stock is on track for its worst weekly performance since early October. In contrast, rivals reported positive Q4 results: General Motors saw a 21% year-over-year U.S. sales increase, Ford rose 9%, and Rivian delivered 14,183 vehicles, exceeding the 13,000-unit consensus estimate.

Nvidia's 2024 valuation surged by over $2 trillion, reaching over $3 trillion. The company plans to introduce Jetson Thor computers for humanoid robots in 2025, positioning itself to compete with Tesla, which is targeting the availability of its Optimus humanoid robot by 2026.

Tesla analysts are divided following a fourth-quarter delivery miss where production fell below delivery figures. Canaccord Genuity's George Gianarikas maintained a Buy rating and raised the price target from $298 to $404, citing long-term growth in EVs, AI, and robotics. GLJ Research's Gordon Johnson maintained a Sell rating with a $24.86 price target, warning of "stormy weather" and potential demand peaking. Truist's William Stein maintained a Hold rating, lowering the price target from $360 to $351 due to inventory burn and downward pressure on average selling prices. Tesla stock recently traded at $389.20.

Options activity for TSLA shows a bullish call option sweep expiring on January 3, 2025. Traders executed 42 contracts at a $390.00 strike price, split across 10 trades. The total trade price was $25.4K, with 108.2K volume and 8.0K open interest recorded at that strike.

Bitcoin celebrated its 16th anniversary on January 3, 2025. The article notes that Tesla Inc. added Bitcoin to its balance sheet in 2021, a year when the cryptocurrency reached an all-time high of $68,789.63. Additionally, Elon Musk has supported the theory that computer scientist Nick Szabo created Bitcoin.

Tesla's 2024 deliveries fell 1% to 1.79 million, the first decline since inception. Q4 2024 deliveries were 496,000, missing estimates of 500,000-510,000. Goldman Sachs lowered 2025 delivery forecasts to 2.01 million (12% growth) and reduced 2024/2025 EPS estimates. Goldman maintains a $345 price target and Neutral rating, noting FSD v13 improvements but limited robotaxi potential in the next 12-24 months.

European automaker stocks like Stellantis and Volkswagen fell after losing U.S. EV tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act. Tesla's Cybertruck now qualifies for the $7,500 tax credit. U.S. EV sales are expected to reach 1.3 million in 2024, with 18 models currently qualifying for credits.
Truist Securities analyst William Stein maintained a Hold rating on Tesla (TSLA) while cutting the price target from $360 to $351. Tesla shares closed at $379.28 on Thursday.

Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) has begun limited risk production of 2nm chips at its Baoshan fab and plans to commercialize 4nm chips in Arizona. Mass production of N2P process technology is set for the second half of 2026. TSM held a 64.9% market share in Q3 2024. Elon Musk praised Taiwan's technical expertise and talent pool.

Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni stated that investments by Elon Musk's companies do not threaten Italy's national security. Discussions between Meloni and Musk have focused on space and AI investments, supported by Italy's new regulatory framework for foreign space companies.

U.S. new-car sales in 2024 are projected to exceed 15.8 million units, a five-year high. While the industry grew, Tesla and Stellantis did not achieve year-over-year sales growth. EV sales are expected to reach 1.3 million, or 8% of total sales. Average incentives per vehicle rose 30.7% from December 2023 to December 2024.

Tesla achieved record sales in China in 2024 with 657,000 vehicles, an 8.8% year-on-year increase, including 83,000 units in December. China accounted for 40% of Tesla's Q4 global deliveries. Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives maintains an Outperform rating and $515 price target. Tesla will report Q4 financial results on Jan. 29, with expected EPS of 74 cents and revenue of $27.385 billion.

U.S. stock futures rose on Friday following a decline in the previous session. Tesla shares were up 1.43% in premarket trading despite a 6% drop on Thursday caused by disappointing fourth-quarter production and delivery figures. Elon Musk donated 268,000 Tesla shares to charity. Other market news includes Lifecore Biomedical rising 5.26% on better-than-expected results, while SPS Commerce fell 3.73% following an acquisition announcement.

Elon Musk donated 268,000 Tesla shares to charity as part of year-end tax planning, according to a December 31 regulatory filing. Based on Tesla's $379.28 closing price on Thursday, the donation is valued at approximately $101.6 million. Musk retains ownership of 410.794 million Tesla shares following the transaction.

U.S. stocks closed lower on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite marking their fifth consecutive day of losses. Tesla shares fell over 6% following the release of fourth-quarter production and delivery data. The CNN Money Fear and Greed index remained in the 'Fear' zone at a reading of 26.1.

Tesla reported a 114% year-over-year increase in energy storage deployments, reaching 31.4 GWh in 2024 compared to 14.7 GWh in 2023. Conversely, the company experienced its first annual decline in vehicle deliveries, delivering 1,789,226 units in 2024, a 1.1% decrease from 2023. Tesla operates a Megafactory in Lathrop, California, and is preparing a new facility in Shanghai.

BYD overtook Tesla as the world's largest battery electric vehicle (BEV) seller in the fourth quarter of 2024, delivering 595,413 units compared to Tesla's 495,570. However, Tesla maintained its lead for the full year 2024 with 1,789,226 deliveries versus BYD's 1,764,992. This marks the first year Tesla's annual deliveries have fallen, down 1.1% from 2023.

Elon Musk stated that a Cybertruck involved in an explosion outside the Trump International Hotel on New Year's Day will be repaired and returned to the road. Musk confirmed the vehicle's battery pack and tires were undamaged and that the incident, which involved fireworks or a bomb in the truck bed, was unrelated to the vehicle itself.

Tesla shares fell 6.08% to close at $379.28 on Thursday, trading between an intraday high of $392.73 and a low of $373.04. The decline followed fourth-quarter delivery figures that missed market expectations. The stock remains well above its 52-week low of $138.80 but below its 52-week high of $488.54.

Tesla reported 495,570 deliveries in 4Q, missing the 506,763 estimate and marking the first annual sales decline with 1,789,200 total deliveries in 2024. Shares fell 6.08% to $379.28. Analysts are divided: Gary Black maintains a $380 price target, citing a potential $25,000-$30,000 vehicle launch in 1H 2025, while Gordon Johnson criticized Tesla's Optimus robot as outdated technology. Truist analyst William Stein noted concerns with FSD v13 performance.

Senator Bernie Sanders criticized Elon Musk's support for the H-1B visa program, arguing it displaces American workers with lower-wage foreign labor. Sanders highlighted that Tesla laid off over 7,500 American workers in 2024 while employing guest workers in roles like associate accountants and mechanical engineers. He cited data suggesting the top 30 H-1B users laid off 85,000 Americans while hiring 34,000 guest workers.

Tesla faced mixed analyst reactions following its first annual sales drop, with Wedbush's Daniel Ives forecasting 20%-30% growth in 2025, while Ross Gerber criticized the company's reliance on discounts. The broader market saw the Nasdaq and S&P 500 end a five-day losing streak, though both indices finished the week lower.

Tesla ranked as the second most-searched ticker on Benzinga Pro in 2024, behind Nvidia. Tesla's stock ended the year with a 62.5% return, trading between $138.80 and $488.54. It held the top search spot in October and December 2024.

Analyst Karl Brauer of iSeeCars stated that Elon Musk's target of 20% to 30% sales growth for Tesla in 2025 is unrealistic, citing long ramp-up times for new models. Tesla reported 495,570 deliveries in 4Q, missing the target required to exceed 2023's 1,808,581 total deliveries, resulting in a 1.1% annual decline. The company plans to launch more affordable vehicles in 1H 2025.

A group of Tesla shareholders, including Ark Investment Management, filed an appeal in the Delaware Supreme Court against the decision to void Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package. Judge Kathaleen St. J. McCormick originally voided the package in January 2024, citing board influence, and ruled against it again in December after shareholders voted to reinstate it in June 2024. Musk praised the appeal, criticizing the original lawsuit brought by Richard Tornetta.

Elon Musk announced an algorithm tweak for X to reduce negativity and promote informational and entertaining content. The goal is to maximize 'unregretted user-seconds.' This follows Musk's acquisition of the platform in October 2022 and his reinstatement of various accounts, including Donald Trump's.

JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman warned that Tesla's profitability could drop by 40% under the second Trump administration due to the potential removal of EV tax credits and subsidies. Tesla reported 1.79 million vehicle sales in 2024, a decline from 1.8 million in 2023. Brinkman projected a $3.2 billion loss for Tesla from subsidy removal, contradicting Musk's view that such changes would benefit the company.

Data from a Tesla Cybertruck involved in a New Year's Day explosion in Las Vegas helped police determine the cause was explosives rather than a vehicle malfunction. The Cybertruck ranked fifth on the list. Cox Automotive forecasts EV market share to reach 10% or more in 2025.

Wall Street rallied on Wednesday as December CPI rose 2.9% year-over-year, fueling expectations for Fed rate cuts in 2025. Financial stocks led the gains following strong Q4 earnings from firms like JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs. Tesla shares climbed over 5% after RBC increased its price target from $313 to $440 and reaffirmed an outperform rating.

Options activity for Tesla on January 15, 2025, showed a bullish call sweep expiring January 17, 2025, with 189 contracts at a $420 strike price. The total trade price was $170.1K, involving 66,013 contracts traded today against 20,804 in prior open interest.

Tesla options activity showed 159 calls and 30 puts, with 45% of investors leaning bullish. Major trades included a $400 call expiring January 17, 2025. Tesla is trading at $408.91, up 3.17%. Five analysts over the past month set an average price target of $350.37, with ratings ranging from a $24 target by GLJ Research to a $492 target by Stifel.

Tesla plans to suspend parts of its Shanghai manufacturing lines for the refreshed Model Y from Jan. 22 to Feb. 14 to prepare for increased production. The Model 3 line will also be closed from Jan. 26 to Feb. 3. The refreshed Model Y, launched in China last week, starts at 263,500 yuan, a 5.4% increase over the previous version, with deliveries expected in March. Tesla reported 1.79 million global deliveries in 2024, down from 1.81 million in 2023, while BYD delivered over 1.76 million BEVs.

Over 1.3 million EVs were sold in the U.S. in 2024, a 7.3% year-on-year increase. Tesla led with the Model Y (372,613 units, 28.6% market share) and Model 3 (189,903 units, 14.6% share). Ford's Mustang Mach-E (51,745 units) and F-150 Lightning (33,510 units) ranked 3rd and 6th. Hyundai's Ioniq 5 sold 44,400 units, while Tesla's Cybertruck sold 38,965 units. Honda's Prologue sold 33,017 units. Cox Automotive expects 2025 to be a record year for EV volume, projecting EVs will account for 10% of total sales.

Rivian deployed vehicles to support relief efforts for California wildfires, providing transport for supplies and offering free DC fast charging to owners within 150 miles of Los Angeles. Tesla and Ford also contributed; Tesla provided Cybertrucks for Starlink connectivity and Megapack chargers for power, while Ford issued grants to the Los Angeles Fire Department Foundation and the California Community Foundation.

In 2024, Hyundai and Kia collectively sold 117,826 EVs in the U.S., surpassing Ford's 97,865 units. Tesla remained the top-selling brand with approximately 634,000 units. GM's brands collectively sold 114,426 EVs. The Ioniq 5 was Hyundai's best-seller with over 44,000 units, ranking fourth overall, while the Tesla Cybertruck ranked fifth, ahead of the Ford F-150 Lightning.

The SEC sued Elon Musk for failing to timely disclose his stake in Twitter (now X) in 2022. The lawsuit alleges that by March 2022, Musk had acquired over 5% of common stock without required disclosure, allowing him to purchase shares at artificially low prices and underpay investors by over $150 million. Musk eventually disclosed a 9% stake in April 2022, causing the stock price to rise by over 27%.

On Jan. 13, 2024, Tesla stock fell 1.72% to $396.36. While Cybertruck sales reached nearly 39,000 units in 2024, sales of the Model S and X declined. Other trending stocks included Microsoft (-0.36%), Applied Digital (+9.98% on $63.9 million revenue), Rigetti Computing (+47.93%), and Eli Lilly (-6.59% after reducing Q4 revenue guidance to $13.5 billion).

Tesla remains the largest holding in the ARKQ ETF at 15.44%. Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney maintained a Neutral rating on Tesla with a $345 price target. Delaney incorporated robotaxi revenue into his models, projecting operations to begin in the second half of 2026 and reach $115 million in 2027 based on a fleet of 1,500 vehicles. He noted improvements in FSD V13 but emphasized that Tesla needs significant progress to meet its goal of being safer than a human driver by 2Q25. The analyst remains cautious, citing full valuation and potential volatility in auto fundamentals and 2025 delivery volumes.

Duffy also pledged to continue safety investigations into Tesla if confirmed. The incoming administration has previously signaled intentions to end the $7,500 EV consumer tax credit.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives identified Tesla CEO Elon Musk and Oracle's Larry Ellison as top contenders to acquire TikTok, which has an estimated valuation of $40-$50 billion. Ives noted that any acquisition would require approval from both Beijing and the Trump administration. President Trump recently suspended the U.S. TikTok ban for 75 days.

Tesla bull Ross Gerber expressed skepticism regarding the effectiveness of executive orders in overriding existing laws related to a potential TikTok ban. The article also mentions that Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) faces a 'choppy' first half of the year due to new EV policies, though some analysts maintain a $500 price target and see 20% upside.

A report cited by Benzinga noted that Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) lost $15 billion in brand value in 2024. Analysts attributed this decline to CEO Elon Musk's political statements and his alignment with President Donald Trump, which may have negatively influenced consumer sentiment.

President Trump's executive order to revoke the EV 'mandate' and the potential repeal of the $7,500 federal tax credit could impact Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) vehicle affordability and sales. While some industry figures remain optimistic about long-term EV progress, the loss of tax incentives poses a challenge for the sector.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk publicly criticized OpenAI CEO Sam Altman on X, calling him a 'liar' regarding a potential $10 billion equity agreement. Musk, a co-founder of OpenAI who left in 2018, has previously labeled the company's transition to a for-profit model a 'total scam' and has engaged in legal disputes with the organization.

Tesla executive Siddhant Awasthi announced that the launch of Cybertruck accessories, including off-road armor and rock sliders, is delayed until the end of March. Additionally, Tesla is currently finishing the delivery of wheel covers, with distribution cited as a bottleneck. In 2024, Tesla delivered 38,965 Cybertrucks in the U.S., making it the fifth best-selling EV in the country.

Researcher Troy Teslike predicts Tesla will deliver 1.8 million vehicles in 2025, matching 2024 levels, contradicting CEO Elon Musk's 20-30% growth forecast. Teslike expects a new, affordable model priced under $30,000 (after a $7,500 federal tax credit) in the second half of 2025. Tesla delivered 1.79 million vehicles in 2024. Recent price adjustments include a 25% increase for the refreshed Model Y in the U.S. and price hikes in China and Canada.

Senator Elizabeth Warren sent a letter to Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) chair Elon Musk proposing federal spending cuts of over $2 trillion. Her recommendations include reducing federal enforcement against marijuana users and businesses, which she labels wasteful. Warren cited FBI data showing marijuana possession arrests account for roughly 25% of all drug possession arrests and noted the DEA eradicated over 5.7 million plants in 2022.

Entrepreneur Mark Cuban defended Tesla CEO Elon Musk's online behavior, describing it as "kind of insane" but consistent with his personality. Cuban noted that while he is willing to criticize Musk when deserved, he views Musk's controversial social media posts as typical banter. Musk has previously faced a $40 million SEC settlement over a tweet and a Secret Service investigation regarding remarks about Donald Trump.

Biographer Seth Abramson labeled Elon Musk a "sociopath" following the re-emergence of images from Musk's January 2024 visit to Auschwitz. The criticism follows recent scrutiny regarding a gesture Musk made after Donald Trump's inauguration, which some observers compared to a Nazi salute, a claim Musk dismissed as propaganda.

In his upcoming memoir, Bill Gates suggests he would likely be diagnosed on the autism spectrum if he were a child today. Gates reflects on his childhood challenges, including missing social cues and obsessive focus on projects. He joins other business leaders like Elon Musk and Richard Branson in discussing neurodivergence.

The EV market faces uncertainty following President Trump's inauguration and potential changes to the $7,500 federal tax credit. Researcher Troy Teslike outlined scenarios ranging from restricted eligibility to total cancellation within six months. Gary Black of The Future Fund expressed concern over the impact of removing incentives on Tesla's sales. Additionally, Elon Musk hinted that FSD-equipped vehicles will soon gain the ability to reverse automatically.

Elon Musk appeared via video at a rally for Germany's far-right AfD party, urging voters to prioritize national self-determination over European Union influence and to move beyond guilt regarding Germany's fascist past. Musk's endorsement of the nationalist, anti-immigration party comes four weeks before German elections and follows recent controversies regarding his public conduct.

Tesla faces a potential boycott in Poland after CEO Elon Musk made comments downplaying Germany's past at a rally for the Alternative for Germany party. Polish Sports and Tourism Minister Slawomir Nitras urged citizens to stop buying Teslas, citing the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz. Tesla is currently the bestselling EV brand in Poland, with 4,460 units sold in 2024. Additionally, Tesla's global brand value fell 26% to $43 billion in 2025, and surveys in the Netherlands indicate that Musk's persona is negatively impacting brand loyalty and consideration.

Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney maintains a Neutral rating on Tesla with a $345 price target ahead of Q4 earnings on January 29. Key items to watch include automotive non-GAAP gross margins, 2025 delivery outlook, FSD progress, and the refreshed Model Y. Tesla is targeting 20% to 30% year-over-year delivery growth for 2025. Delaney noted that Tesla's U.S. market share dropped to the mid-40% range in Q4, while European market share fell to 13%. The analyst also highlighted concerns regarding the potential end of the $7,500 federal tax credit and the timeline for the Cybercab robotaxi.

Siddharth Singhai, CIO of IronHold Capital, identified the U.S. auto industry as a sector to watch, noting that protectionist policies under the Trump administration could benefit General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis by mitigating cost disadvantages against Chinese EVs. Singhai suggested investors be cautious of auto companies vulnerable to the EV transition. The First Trust S-Network Future Vehicles & Tech ETF (CARZ), which includes Tesla, Ford, GM, and Stellantis, is highlighted as a tool for exposure to domestic automotive manufacturing growth.

Elon Musk has previously targeted 2025 for low-volume production of Optimus for internal use, with potential sales prices between $10,000 and $20,000.

Citron Research founder Andrew Left filed a petition with the SEC seeking protection for free speech in online stock market discussions. The petition, filed by Dynamis LLP, argues that current regulatory actions create a chilling effect on market participation. Left, who faced SEC fraud charges in July 2024 regarding his commentary on stocks including Tesla, Nvidia, and Meta, is requesting clear guidelines on trading windows, disclaimer safe harbors, and the scope of trading restrictions. The petition notes that Citron suffered significant losses during the 2021 GameStop short squeeze.

Tesla received over 70,000 orders for its refreshed Model Y in China within five days of its launch. He noted that while Nvidia faces high risk from this shift, Tesla remains the least exposed among major tech stocks. Cybercab volume production is targeted for 2026. Tesla ended the quarter with $1.07 billion in digital assets.

While Tesla removed ultrasonic sensors in 2022 and relies solely on cameras, critics like Zoox co-founder Jesse Levinson argue that additional hardware is necessary for safety. Waymo continues to utilize LiDAR in its robotaxis.

The company expects energy storage deployment to grow at least 50% year-over-year in 2025. Conversely, automotive revenue dipped 8% to $19.8 billion. Tesla operates a Megafactory in Lathrop, California, and is building another in Shanghai, expected to ship Megapacks in Q1 2025.

CEO Elon Musk stated that Tesla will likely need to upgrade the Hardware 3 computers of customers who purchased FSD to achieve fully unsupervised autonomous driving. The company reported Q4 operating expenses of $2.60 billion, a 9% year-on-year increase, and 2024 capital expenditure of $11.34 billion. Despite missing Q4 revenue and earnings estimates, the stock rose up to 5% after-hours on promises of new models and autonomous vehicle deployment.

Tesla stock rose 5% after-hours following promises to launch 'more affordable' models in the first half of 2025 and plans to license FSD software to other automakers. The company plans to start testing autonomous ride-hailing in Texas in June and aims to launch unsupervised FSD in California by the end of 2025. Tesla reported Q4 revenue, EPS, and gross margins below expectations.

Tesla bull Gary Black praised the planned June launch of paid autonomous ride-hailing in Austin as the most bullish takeaway from the Q4 earnings call. However, he expressed concerns regarding low automotive gross margins of 13.6% (excluding regulatory credits) and the form factor of the upcoming $30,000-$35,000 vehicle, warning that it must not cannibalize existing sales. Tesla reported Q4 revenue of $25.71 billion, missing the $27.26 billion estimate, and a 2024 delivery decline to 1.79 million vehicles. The company plans new vehicle production in the first half of 2025 and Cybercab volume production in 2026.

A group of U.S. investors, including MrBeast and Roblox CEO David Baszucki, has secured over $20 billion for a potential TikTok takeover. While ByteDance has stated TikTok's U.S. business is not for sale, other potential contenders mentioned include Microsoft, Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, and Tesla CEO Elon Musk. Analyst Dan Ives estimates a potential sale value between $40 billion and $50 billion.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk announced plans to produce several thousand Optimus humanoid robots this year for internal factory use, with a next-generation version slated for launch mid-next year for sale to rival companies. Musk projected long-term revenue potential for Optimus to exceed $10 trillion. While production costs are expected to drop below $20,000 at scale, pricing will be market-driven. Tesla also confirmed the start of production for new, more affordable vehicle models in the first half of 2025.

Tesla reported an unrealized gain of $600 million on its cryptocurrency holdings in Q4, following a new accounting standard that allows firms to report holdings at market value. Tesla's digital assets on its balance sheet rose to $1.07 billion, with holdings of 11,509 BTC. This gain accounted for 26% of the company's total income for the quarter. Tesla reported Q4 revenue of $25.71 billion, up 2% year-over-year.

In a Benzinga reader poll, 39% of respondents identified Tesla as the Magnificent 7 stock most negatively impacted by the Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates steady at 4.25% to 4.5% in January. The poll suggests this is due to Tesla's higher-priced products and sensitivity to borrowing rates. Economists currently predict the first rate cut may not occur until June or later.

Major U.S. stock indices recovered from Monday's losses, with tech stocks leading the rally on Friday. Tesla and Amazon were primary drivers of the Magnificent Seven's 1.6% gain. The S&P 500 reached 6,118 points, nearing its all-time high. Meanwhile, gold prices exceeded $2,800 per ounce, and Bitcoin held steady at $104,800.

STMicroelectronics, a supplier to Tesla and Apple, is considering cutting up to 3,000 jobs (6% of its workforce) in Italy and France due to a demand slump in the auto and industrial sectors. The company reported Q4 revenue of $3.32 billion, beating estimates, but expects Q1 revenue of $2.51 billion, which is below analyst expectations. Gross margins fell to 37.7%.

In today's session, a bullish call option sweep for TSLA was observed with a $420.00 strike price expiring on 01/31/25. The trade involved 410 contracts split into 23 trades, totaling $73.6K with a price of $183.0 per contract. Open interest was 19,083, and total volume was 155,641.

Toyota remained the world's best-selling automaker in 2024 with 10.8 million sales, a 4% decline. Toyota's electrified vehicle sales reached 4.5 million, but it sold only 139,892 battery electric vehicles (BEVs). In contrast, Tesla delivered nearly 1.79 million BEVs and BYD delivered 1.76 million in 2024.

Gary Black of The Future Fund stated that Uber will likely remain the largest ride-hailing player by offering autonomous rides. Uber has a partnership with Waymo to dispatch autonomous Jaguar I-PACE vehicles in Atlanta later this year. Tesla CEO Elon Musk announced plans to test autonomous ride-hailing in Texas in June using Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology.

The Dow Jones rose over 150 points on Thursday as investors assessed earnings. Tesla reported weaker-than-expected Q4 earnings and sales but expects vehicle business growth in 2025. President Trump confirmed a 25% tariff on imports from Canada and Mexico starting Feb. 1. The Fear & Greed Index remained in the 'Neutral' zone at 48.1.

Vivek Ramaswamy clarified his departure from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), citing a strategic pivot toward elected office, potentially a bid for Ohio's governorship. He emphasized that government reform should be pursued through legislation and expressed his intent to address regulatory challenges at the state level.

Tesla has marked the white interior option as sold out for the launch series of the refreshed Model Y in the U.S. before deliveries begin in March. The launch series is priced at $59,990. CEO Elon Musk noted that factories will begin producing the new Model Y in February, warning that retooling and ramp-related costs will impact margins.

Tesla's employee headcount was 125,665 as of December 31, 2024, a 10.5% year-on-year decrease following an April 2024 layoff of 10% of its global workforce. Tesla also reported 1.79 million global EV deliveries in 2024, down from 1.81 million in 2023. The company plans to test autonomous ride-hailing in Texas in June using FSD technology.

Researcher Troy Teslike forecasts a 1% decline in Tesla's 2025 deliveries to 1.77 million units, below the analyst consensus of over 2 million. Teslike expects sales to drop in the U.S. and Europe, though rise in China. Tesla plans to start production of new, affordable models in the first half of 2025, but Teslike warns these may cannibalize existing sales. CEO Elon Musk noted that retooling factories for the refreshed Model Y will cause several weeks of lost production in Q1. Tesla stock closed at $400.28 on Thursday, with a consensus price target of $320.75.

Tesla stock rose 2.87% to close at $400.28 on January 30, 2025, with a 52-week range between $138.80 and $488.54. CEO Elon Musk announced that production of the new Model Y will result in several weeks of lost production during the first quarter.
On January 30, 2025, Tesla shares rose 2.87% as investors parsed earnings from 'Magnificent 7' companies. The broader market also saw gains, with the Nasdaq Composite closing at 19,681.75.

Investor Ross Gerber criticized Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, citing 'severe hardware limitations' and praising competitor Waymo's autonomous technology. Gerber noted that Tesla's vision-only approach struggles with environmental factors like dust, ash, and sun glare. Tesla is currently under an NHTSA investigation regarding 2.6 million FSD-equipped vehicles, while CEO Elon Musk maintains that FSD will exceed human driving safety by Q2 2025.

Palantir CEO Alex Karp discussed the competitive threat of China's open-source AI model, DeepSeek, and emphasized the need for U.S. AI development. Karp also defended Tesla CEO Elon Musk against political allegations.

JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman expressed confusion over Tesla's 8% stock surge following an earnings miss, where Q4 revenue of $25.7 billion and EPS of $0.73 both missed estimates. Brinkman maintains a $135 price target, citing a 68% potential downside, and argues that the stock's performance is 'divorced from the fundamentals' despite Musk's ambitious growth projections for 2025.

Tesla has begun global production of the refreshed Model Y across its four factories in Berlin, Shanghai, Texas, and Fremont. Deliveries are scheduled to start in March in both China and the U.S. CEO Elon Musk noted that retooling for this simultaneous global update will cause several weeks of lost production in Q1 and impact margins. Tesla reported 1.79 million global deliveries in 2024, down from 1.81 million in 2023.

Gary Black suggests a corporate tax rate reduction from 21% to 15% could increase Tesla's FY25 EPS by $0.12, or 4%. Ross Gerber criticized Tesla's Full Self-Driving, favoring Waymo's robotaxi technology. Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe stated that eliminating the $7,500 EV tax credit would be a minor speed bump for the sector. A Polish official called for a Tesla boycott due to Elon Musk's comments at a German political rally. Musk announced plans to produce several thousand Optimus humanoid robots this year for internal factory use, with long-term revenue potential estimated at over $10 trillion.

Tesla, valued at $1.24 trillion, reported FY2024 net income of $8.4 billion and EPS of $2.42, resulting in a P/E ratio of 159.0. In contrast, JPMorgan Chase, with a $749.15 billion market cap, reported $58.5 billion in net income and $19.75 EPS, yielding a P/E of 13.6. Analysts and investors like Cathie Wood argue Tesla should be valued as a high-growth tech and autonomous taxi platform company rather than a traditional automaker, citing potential in AI, robotics, and FSD.

Tesla's 2024 sales in California dropped 12% overall and 8% in Q4, with Model 3 registrations falling 36% year-over-year. Tesla's U.S. market share declined from 55% in 2023 to 48.7% in 2024, with 633,762 total vehicles sold, a 5.6% decrease. Brand Finance reported Tesla's brand value fell $15 billion in 2024, dropping from 18th to 36th place in 2025. Reports suggest consumer sentiment may be impacted by Elon Musk's political support for Donald Trump.

Tesla shares fell 6% to $380.19 on Monday following President Trump's announcement of 25% tariffs on Canadian and Mexican imports and 10% on Chinese goods, though the Mexico tariff was later paused for one month. The EV industry faces potential cost increases for components like batteries and semiconductors. Tesla's Q4 2024 results showed adjusted EPS of 73 cents (missing the 76-cent estimate) and $25.70 billion in revenue (missing the $27.26 billion estimate), with a 7% year-over-year decline in production.

Tesla shares declined 6% on Monday amid broader market volatility triggered by President Trump's announcement of tariffs on Mexico, Canada, and China. While the Mexico tariff was suspended for one month following a pledge to increase border security, concerns regarding trade relations with China and potential retaliation weighed on tech-sensitive stocks. Other Magnificent Seven members, including Apple and Nvidia, also saw declines of 4% and 3.8%, respectively.

Benzinga's options activity scanner identified a bearish put option sweep for TSLA expiring on February 7, 2025, with a $380.00 strike price. The trade involved 24 contracts across 11 trades, totaling $26.6K in premium, with 38,990 contracts traded today.

Researcher Troy Teslike expects Tesla to import Model Y vehicles into Canada from its Berlin gigafactory due to new 25% retaliatory tariffs on U.S. imports effective February 4, 2025. Tesla previously stopped importing from China after Canada increased tariffs on Chinese EVs to 100% on October 1, 2024. Tesla sold 48,000 cars in Canada in 2024.

Tesla shares fell 2.4% to $394.80 in pre-market trading on Monday following President Trump's announcement of 25% tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada and 10% on imports from China. Former executive Rohan Patel criticized the tariff policy.

Former Tesla executive Rohan Patel criticized President Trump's 25% tariffs on Mexico and Canada and 10% on China as a 'trade bomb' that creates global distrust. Automakers including Tesla and GM are expected to be impacted. Tesla previously postponed plans for a Mexican gigafactory due to tariff concerns. S&P Global Mobility noted a 25% duty could add $6,250 to the cost of vehicles from Mexico and Canada.

Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Andres Sheppard maintained a Neutral rating on Tesla and raised the price target from $365 to $425 on January 30, projecting over 8% upside. The company recently announced it has started production of its refreshed Model Y across its four global factories, including the Texas gigafactory.

Venture capitalist Chamath Palihapitiya has moved the incorporation of his last four companies to Nevada, citing concerns over Delaware's 'judicial activism' against Tesla shareholders. This follows a broader trend of companies relocating from states like California and Delaware to business-friendly environments like Texas and Nevada.

Former Greek Finance Minister Yanis Varoufakis warns that the TikTok ban highlights the excessive power of tech platform owners, whom he calls 'techno-feudal lords.' He advocates for 'interoperability' regulations to allow users to transfer followers between platforms. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives estimates a potential TikTok joint venture could be worth up to $50 billion, with potential buyers including Microsoft, Oracle, and a consortium led by MrBeast. Meanwhile, Meta has invested over $2 billion in creator payments in 2024 to attract users to Instagram Reels.

TSMC shares fell 6.6% to 1,070 New Taiwan dollars following a global AI stock selloff triggered by Chinese firm DeepSeek. DeepSeek claims its R1 model cost $5.6 million to develop, a figure Wedbush analyst Dan Ives dismissed as likely fictional compared to the $100 million-plus cost of OpenAI's GPT-4. Concerns have been raised by figures like Tesla CEO Elon Musk and Scale AI CEO Alexandr Wang regarding DeepSeek's potential use of U.S. technology and acquisition of advanced chips. The White House is investigating the company for national security risks.

Investor Gary Black warns that Tesla's global export strategy is at risk due to rising trade tensions and high interest rates. President Trump's 25% tariffs on Canadian and Mexican imports, effective Feb. 1, could trigger a retaliatory trade war. Data from Troy Teslike shows Tesla exported 323,499 vehicles in 2024, or 18.1% of its 1.79 million total deliveries, with the Shanghai factory accounting for 246,910 of those exports. The Federal Reserve maintains interest rates between 4.25% and 4.5%, keeping borrowing costs high for consumers.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives compares buying Palantir stock today to buying Tesla stock in 2015, suggesting Palantir could reach a $1 trillion market cap. Palantir reported its sixth consecutive quarter of beating analyst estimates, with 36% year-over-year revenue growth and 52% growth in the U.S. The company closed 129 deals worth over $1 million and 58 deals worth at least $5 million in the fourth quarter. Palantir shares rose 22.5% to $102.53 following the report.

President Trump's executive order to create a U.S. Sovereign Wealth Fund (SWF) has sparked debate over its structure and potential investments. Unlike a Bitcoin-only reserve, the SWF could include equities, bonds, real estate, and commodities. Experts suggest the fund might invest in technology companies like Nvidia and Tesla, or even acquire stakes in entities like TikTok for national security. Challenges include the U.S.'s status as a net debtor, with experts noting that deficit reduction, potentially via the DOGE initiative, is required to generate surplus capital for investment.

Benzinga's options activity scanner identified a bearish call option sweep for TSLA expiring on February 7, 2025. Tesla launched non-foundation series Cybertrucks in Canada, with the all-wheel drive variant starting at C$114,990 and the Cyberbeast at C$142,990. Simultaneously, Tesla increased prices for the foundation series to C$153,990 for the all-wheel drive and C$181,990 for the Cyberbeast, marking an 11.6% hike. This follows recent price increases across Tesla's Canadian vehicle lineup.

Researcher Troy Teslike predicts Tesla will sell 21,000 Cybertrucks in 2025, significantly below the 65,000 units estimated by Wall Street analysts. Teslike noted 34,426 deliveries in 2024 and claimed the order backlog is exhausted, with 10,600 unsold units at year-end. He dismissed the likelihood of a cheaper variant this year, citing lack of profitability.

Tesla announced that the 2025 all-wheel drive Cybertruck qualifies for a $7,500 federal EV tax credit, reducing the effective price to $72,490 for eligible customers with purchases under $80,000. The Cyberbeast variant remains ineligible due to its $99,990 starting price. While the single-motor variant is also eligible, production has not yet commenced.

Tesla's vehicle registrations in California fell 11.6% in 2024 to 203,221, marking five consecutive quarters of decline. Tesla's share of the state's zero-emission vehicle market dropped to 52.5% from 60.1% in 2023. Globally, Tesla reported 1.79 million deliveries in 2024, down from 1.81 million in 2023.

Short seller Jim Chanos criticized federal subsidies received by Tesla following the news that the Cybertruck qualifies for a $7,500 tax incentive. This comes amid declining sales in California, where registrations dropped 12% in 2024, and reports that Tesla's brand value fell by $15 billion in 2024. Tesla stock closed at $383.68 on Monday, down 5.17%.

Tesla stock fell 5.17% to close at $383.68, with an intraday range of $374.36 to $389.17. The decline is attributed to the imposition of new tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China. The stock's 52-week range is $138.80 to $488.54.

The White House has appointed Elon Musk as a 'special government employee' to lead the Department of Government Efficiency. This role grants him access to government systems and allows him to potentially bypass standard financial disclosure rules. President Trump stated that Musk cannot act without White House approval. The initiative aims for a $4 billion-a-day cost-cutting plan.

Negotiations for the sale of TikTok's U.S. operations are at an impasse as China resists a forced sale amid trade tensions and new U.S. tariffs. President Trump has suggested he is open to Tesla CEO Elon Musk buying TikTok. Potential buyers include various tech entrepreneurs and companies, with a minimum price tag of $50 billion.

Tesla options activity shows a bearish sentiment, with 65% of 23 unusual trades being bearish. Big players are targeting a price range of $375 to $400. Tesla stock is currently down 1.34% at $386.94. Five professional analysts in the last 30 days set an average price target of $457, with ratings ranging from Neutral to Outperform/Overweight.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi may meet with Tesla CEO Elon Musk on February 13 during his U.S. visit. Musk has previously discussed launching Tesla vehicles and Starlink in India, though the meeting is not expected to result in an immediate announcement regarding manufacturing in the country.

Tesla launched a limited-time 8,000 yuan ($1,100) insurance subsidy for new Model 3 purchases in China, valid for orders placed before February 28. Researcher Troy Teslike expects this to have a limited impact on Tesla's delivery challenges. In 2024, Tesla's global deliveries fell to 1.79 million vehicles, the first decline in over a decade.

Palantir CEO Alex Karp expressed enthusiasm for Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), viewing it as a positive 'revolution' for his company. Palantir reported a 36% year-over-year revenue increase in Q4 2025, with U.S. government contracts contributing $343 million. Analyst Dan Ives compared buying Palantir stock now to buying Tesla in 2015.

Figure AI is ending its collaboration with OpenAI following a 'major breakthrough' in its in-house robot AI, with plans to showcase a new humanoid capability within 30 days. This development parallels Tesla's progress with its Optimus humanoid robot, which Tesla intends to produce in the thousands this year for factory deployment.

GLJ Research CEO Gordon Johnson criticized Tesla bulls, arguing the company is primarily a car manufacturer, noting that automotive revenue accounted for 77% of its 4Q24 total revenue of $25.71 billion. Johnson highlighted a decline in California vehicle registrations, which fell 11.6% in 2024, and cited Tesla's 2024 global delivery drop to 1.79 million vehicles. GLJ Research maintains a 'sell' rating with a $24.86 price target, while Wedbush Securities holds an 'outperform' rating with a $550 target. The consensus price target for Tesla is $320.75.

On February 4, 2025, Tesla stock rose 2.22% to close at $392.21, with an intraday range between $381.40 and $394. The company recently introduced a cheaper Cybertruck version in Canada while increasing prices for its Foundation Series.

The Treasury announced $125 billion in securities to refund $106 billion in maturing Treasuries, with Secretary Scott Bessent emphasizing a focus on the 10-year yield to manage borrowing costs. In early trading, money flows were negative for Tesla, Apple, and Alphabet, while positive for Amazon, Meta, and NVIDIA. Arm Holdings stock fell on guidance that missed whisper numbers, raising concerns about AI growth, despite some Wall Street firms raising price targets.

Options activity for Tesla (TSLA) showed a bullish call option sweep expiring on February 7, 2025, with a $380.00 strike price. The trade involved 200 contracts at a total cost of $35.2K, split across 7 trades, with 52,533 contracts traded in total for the day.

Palantir Technologies has added xAI's large language model Grok to its AIP platform. Grok joins other LLMs like Llama, Gemini, GPT-4, and Claude. Palantir CEO Alex Karp expressed optimism regarding the Musk-led Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). Wedbush analyst Dan Ives suggested that buying Palantir stock now could be comparable to buying Tesla stock in 2015, projecting a potential $1 trillion market cap.

Tesla experienced significant sales declines in several European markets in January, including a 59.5% drop in Germany, 63.4% in France, and 75.4% in Spain. The overall drop for these combined markets was 50.4% year-over-year. Analysts suggest these declines may be linked to the Model Y refresh and CEO Elon Musk's political activities, including his support for the Alternative for Germany party. Tesla's brand value reportedly declined by $15 billion in 2024.

BYD plans to hire 20,000 additional workers in Zhengzhou during the first quarter to increase production capacity. The Zhengzhou facility produced 545,000 vehicles last year, a 169.8% increase. BYD reported 47.5% growth in new energy passenger vehicle sales in January and surpassed Tesla in UK vehicle registrations for the same month. Citi Research named BYD as its top EV stock pick for 2025.

Chinese technology stocks are rallying, driven by the integration of DeepSeek's AI model into platforms like Alibaba's cloud division. The Hang Seng Tech Index has rallied over 10% in two weeks. The average price-to-earnings ratio for the 30 companies in the Hang Seng Tech Index is 20.5, compared to 41.4 for the U.S. 'Magnificent Seven' stocks, which include Tesla.

BYD overtook Tesla in UK new vehicle registrations in January, with 1,614 registrations compared to Tesla's 1,458. Tesla's UK registrations fell from 1,581 in January 2024. Globally, BYD reclaimed the title of the world's largest battery electric vehicle (BEV) seller in the fourth quarter of 2024, delivering 595,413 BEVs compared to Tesla's 495,570.

Tesla's Shanghai Megafactory is scheduled to start production on February 11. This is the company's second factory dedicated to energy storage products, following the Lathrop, California facility. In Q4, Tesla's energy generation and storage revenue doubled to over $3 billion, with 11.0 GWh of storage deployed. The company expects energy storage deployment to grow at least 50% year-over-year in 2025.

Tesla is hiring engineers and manufacturing technicians to ramp up production of its Optimus humanoid robot at its Fremont Factory. CEO Elon Musk stated the company aims to produce several thousand units this year for internal factory use. Musk projected that once production reaches one million units annually, the cost per robot will be below $20,000, and suggested the product has a long-term revenue potential exceeding $10 trillion.

Tesla stock fell 3.58% to close at $378.17, with a 52-week range of $138.80 to $488.54. Reports indicate Elon Musk may meet with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi this month to discuss potential Tesla and Starlink launches in India.

Lucid Group reported 665 total vehicle deliveries in January, including 50 Gravity SUVs and 615 Air sedans, marking a 51% year-over-year increase. The Gravity SUV, starting at $94,900, competes with the Tesla Model X and Rivian R1S. Lucid plans a lower-priced Gravity version at $79,900 later this year.

Tesla faces reputational challenges and declining market share, including a 59.5% drop in German sales in January and an 11.5% year-over-year decline in China-made vehicle sales. Insider selling by Kimbal Musk and others has occurred. Statistical analysis suggests potential for a long position if volatility remains managed, with a 330/415 bull call spread suggested for March 7 expiration.

Options activity for Tesla (TSLA) showed a bearish call option sweep expiring February 7, 2025. Traders traded 200 contracts at a $370.00 strike price, totaling $30.4K in cost, with 72,255 contracts bought and sold.

Tesla stock is down 23% in 2025, trading at $374.32. The company is focusing on the Optimus humanoid robot, targeting production costs below $20,000 per unit. Catalysts include FSD expansion, new Model Y production, and summer Cybercab launches. However, January sales in China dropped 11.5%, and European competition from BYD is increasing.

Tesla executives and directors sold over $73 million in stock recently. Board chair Robyn M. Denholm sold 112,390 shares for $43.2 million, and CFO Vaibhav Taneja sold 7,000 shares for $2.8 million, both under pre-existing trading plans. Kimbal Musk sold 75,000 shares for $27.6 million on February 6.

Tesla sold 63,238 China-made vehicles in January, an 11.5% decrease from January 2024. The decline is attributed to the Chinese New Year holidays and production line suspensions for the refreshed Model Y and Model 3. In contrast, rival BYD saw a 47.5% increase in new energy passenger vehicle sales. Tesla shares fell 1.5% in premarket trading.

Mercedes-Benz EV drivers in the U.S. can now access over 20,000 Tesla Supercharger locations using an adapter. Tesla is adding 500-1,000 Supercharger stalls monthly in North America to meet rising demand. As of the end of 2024, Tesla had 65,495 global supercharger connectors, a 19% increase from 2023.

Tesla launched a 0.99% APR financing promotion for Model 3 purchases in Canada for orders placed by March 7 and delivered by April 1, applicable to loan terms up to 48 months. This follows recent price hikes in Canada where Model 3 prices increased by up to C$9,000, bringing the starting price to C$59,990.

Tesla increased the price of the Model X by $5,000, with the all-wheel drive version now priced at $84,990 and the Plaid variant at $99,990. This price hike makes the base Model X ineligible for the $7,500 federal EV tax credit, which requires a price below $80,000.

Future Fund's Gary Black argues that Tesla's autonomous network will not make Uber obsolete, citing Uber's 170 million monthly active users as a significant competitive advantage. Tesla aims to launch a robotaxi service in 2025 in Texas and California, while Uber has partnered with Waymo to offer driverless rides.

Tesla shares declined 1.02% to $374.32, with a 52-week range of $138.80 to $488.54. The drop is linked to reports of a 60% sales decline in Germany, potentially influenced by CEO Elon Musk's political activities.

Tesla experienced a 59.5% year-over-year sales decline in Germany during January, attributed to the Model Y refresh and potential backlash against Elon Musk's political stance, even as the broader German EV market grew by 53.5%.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk's net worth declined by $37 billion between January 18 and February 6, falling from $449 billion to $412 billion, marking the largest decrease among the world's top billionaires during the first three weeks of the Trump administration.

Tesla researcher Troy Teslike criticized Tesla's approach to autonomy, arguing the company is skipping a necessary step before achieving fully driverless operations. Teslike cited Waymo's three-year transition (2017-2020) from supervised to driverless as a benchmark. Tesla CEO Elon Musk plans to begin testing autonomous ride-hailing in Austin, Texas, in June with FSD-equipped vehicles, aiming for multi-city operations by year-end.

Tesla is offering free Supercharging for the duration of ownership to customers who purchase or lease a new Model S or Model X in the U.S., Canada, Puerto Rico, Europe, and the Middle East. This follows a 11.5% year-over-year decline in January sales of China-made vehicles (63,238 units). Tesla also reported a 2024 global delivery total of 1.79 million vehicles, down from 1.81 million in 2023, and expects production of a refreshed Model Y to cause several weeks of lost output in Q1, with deliveries starting in March.

Elon Musk's management style, characterized by high expectations and rapid decision-making, is causing friction among federal workers as he takes on a role at the U.S. DOGE Service. The Washington Post reported that his approach, previously applied at Tesla, SpaceX, and Twitter, contrasts with the stability valued in the federal workforce. The report highlighted the firing of Twitter employee Eric Frohnhoefer as an example of Musk's leadership style.

Tesla is hiring for Optimus robot production, with roles based primarily in California. Additionally, researcher Troy Teslike predicts Tesla will begin shipping Model Y vehicles from its Berlin gigafactory to Canada to avoid U.S. import tariffs. Other mobility news includes Lucid Group reporting a 51% increase in January deliveries and Figure AI announcing a breakthrough in its in-house robot AI.

President Trump announced plans for a U.S. sovereign wealth fund. Meanwhile, economist Jason Furman analyzed Elon Musk's DOGE fiscal targets, estimating that Musk's proposed spending cuts would total approximately $1.5 trillion annually. Additionally, the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget warned that Trump's proposed tax plan could reduce federal revenue by up to $11.2 trillion over the next decade.

Elon Musk has been appointed as a 'special government employee' to lead the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). The initiative has faced scrutiny, including a legal dispute regarding staffer Marko Elez's access to Treasury Department data and his subsequent resignation. Separately, President Trump has imposed tariffs on Canada, China, and Mexico.

Marvell Technology's stock has risen 350% over five years, driven by demand for data infrastructure in AI and automotive markets. Tesla is mentioned as a trillion-dollar company supported by EVs and autonomous driving technology. Marvell reported a 7% revenue decline in fiscal 2024, though analysts like JPMorgan's Harlan Sur expect the company to exceed its $2.5 billion AI revenue target for calendar 2025 due to new ASIC programs for Amazon and Google.

Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) has seen its stock surge 256% over the last five years, reaching a trillion-dollar valuation by capitalizing on the AI boom. The company manufactures chips for major firms including Tesla, Nvidia, and Apple. TSM expects Q1 2025 revenue between $25 billion and $25.8 billion, despite impacts from a January 2025 earthquake. The company reported Q4 revenue of $26.88 billion, up 38.8%, with gross margins at 59%. TSM has committed $38 billion–$42 billion in fiscal 2025 capex and plans significant domestic expansion, including 1nm and 1.4nm fabs in Taiwan and continued production in the U.S. and Germany.

Over the last three months, 36 analysts have provided ratings for Tesla (TSLA), with 7 bullish, 11 somewhat bullish, 11 indifferent, 1 somewhat bearish, and 6 bearish. The average 12-month price target is $362.72, ranging from a low of $24.86 to a high of $550.00. Tesla reported 2024 global deliveries of nearly 1.8 million vehicles. Financial metrics as of December 31, 2024, include a 2.15% revenue growth rate, a 9.0% net margin, a 3.24% return on equity, and a 1.91% return on assets. The company's debt-to-equity ratio is 0.19.
Stifel analyst Stephen Gengaro maintained a Buy rating on Tesla (TSLA) while lowering the price target from $492 to $474. Tesla shares closed at $361.62 on the preceding Friday.

BofA Securities upgraded Mobileye Global Inc (MBLY) from Underperform to Neutral and raised its price target from $12 to $19. Analyst John P. Babcock cited potential contract wins in 2025 as catalysts, though earnings growth is not expected to inflect positively until 2027. In the current session, a bullish put option sweep for TSLA was recorded with an expiration date of February 14, 2025. The trade involved 102 contracts at a $350.00 strike price, totaling $47.9K in cost, with 45,724 contracts traded today.

Potential tariffs on Canadian and Mexican auto imports could increase car prices by $3,000, while proposed tariffs on Chinese graphite, a critical battery component, could rise up to 920%. JPMorgan warns these costs, combined with potential loss of the $7,500 EV tax credit, may slow U.S. EV adoption and squeeze margins for companies like Tesla.

Tesla researcher Troy Teslike questioned the readiness of Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology after a Cybertruck crashed into a light pole in Nevada. Teslike noted the lack of a dedicated rain sensor and difficulties with object detection in poor visibility. The NHTSA is currently investigating 2.4 million Tesla vehicles regarding FSD performance in reduced visibility conditions.

State-owned Chinese automakers Dongfeng Motor Group and Chongqing Changan Automobile are considering a merger that could surpass BYD in market dominance. In 2024, their combined sales reached 5.16 million vehicles compared to BYD's 4.27 million. Meanwhile, BYD reported a 551% surge in UK EV registrations for January, while Tesla saw a 7.79% decline.

Tesla shares fell 1.2% to $357.16 in Monday's pre-market trading. CEO Elon Musk and VP of Finance Sendil Palani addressed tax evasion allegations, stating the company utilized an IRS provision to carry forward losses.

Lucid CEO Peter Rawlinson aims to shift his company's business model to 80% licensing and 20% car manufacturing, similar to an 'Intel inside' strategy. This mirrors Elon Musk's efforts to position Tesla as an AI and robotics company, including plans to license its Full Self-Driving technology to rivals, though automotive revenue still accounted for nearly 79% of Tesla's 2024 revenue.

The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) suspended operations after acting director Russell Vought halted investigations and supervisory activities, citing an 'excessive' $711.6 million balance. This follows Elon Musk's call to dissolve the agency. The suspension impacts ongoing enforcement actions against companies including Equifax, Honda, Block, Capital One, Walmart, Rocket Companies, and major banks like JPMorgan and Bank of America. While some tech leaders support the dissolution, Senator Chris Van Hollen stated the CFPB is protected by law and vowed to fight for its continuation.

Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis praised DeepSeek's AI model as 'probably the best work' from China but stated it lacks significant new scientific advances, relying on known techniques. Hassabis questioned DeepSeek's low-cost claims and chip usage. While DeepSeek's launch fueled a surge in Chinese tech stocks, Hassabis and Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai maintain that Google's Gemini models remain superior in efficiency and performance. Hassabis estimates AGI could be achieved within five years.

Bitcoin advocate Balaji Srinivasan and Senator Mike Lee are advocating for Ron Paul to lead the Federal Reserve, a move supported by Elon Musk. The discussion centers on Paul's 'End the Fed' philosophy. While Musk previously expressed interest in having Paul join the proposed Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), Paul has indicated he does not seek an official position. The initiative aims for federal spending cuts, though experts project more modest savings than the targeted $2 trillion.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk and VP of Finance Sendil Palani addressed tax fraud allegations on X, stating the company complies with global tax regulations. They explained that Tesla does not pay federal income tax due to net operating loss carry-forwards resulting from years of unprofitability. Tesla reported a 2024 net income of $7.09 billion and total revenue of $97.69 billion, with earnings per share of $2.04, down 53% from the previous year.

Elon Musk has publicly dismissed rumors of a TikTok acquisition, stating he prefers to build companies from scratch and has not placed a bid. President Donald Trump recently signed an executive order delaying the enforcement of a ban on the app. While Trump previously expressed openness to a potential deal involving Musk or Oracle's Larry Ellison, China has reportedly taken a firm stance against a forced sale of TikTok's U.S. operations.

A Florida software engineer reported that his Tesla Cybertruck crashed into a curb and a light post while using Full Self-Driving (FSD) version 13.2.4. The driver noted the vehicle failed to slow down or exit the lane. This incident follows an ongoing NHTSA investigation into 2.4 million Tesla vehicles regarding FSD performance in low-visibility conditions and concerns that Tesla's marketing may encourage driver complacency.

Tesla investor Ross Gerber criticized Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) version 13.2.6 on the Cybertruck, calling it 'not usable' and inferior to Waymo. While some users reported better experiences, researcher Troy Teslike questioned Tesla's development approach. Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney maintains a Neutral rating and $345 price target, forecasting $115 million in robotaxi revenue by 2027. The NHTSA is currently investigating 2.6 million Tesla vehicles equipped with FSD.

Tesla shares fell 5.60% to $331.17 on Tuesday after competitor BYD announced plans to make advanced smart driving a standard feature across its lineup, including in a vehicle priced under 70,000 yuan. BYD also announced an AI partnership with DeepSeek. Meanwhile, Tesla confirmed it remains on track to launch autonomous ride-hailing services in Austin in June, though FSD remains unapproved in China due to local restrictions.

Lyft is set to report Q4 2024 earnings with analyst expectations of 22 cents per share and $1.56 billion in revenue. The company plans to deploy Mobileye-powered robotaxis in Dallas by 2026. JPMorgan analyst Samik Chatterjee noted the move as a competitive step against Uber and Tesla, though Lyft still lacks an original equipment manufacturer partner for its vehicles.

Tesla shares dropped over 4% to an 11-week low on Tuesday, pressured by weak January auto sales in China. The broader U.S. market saw mixed results as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled no immediate interest rate cuts, while investors awaited January Consumer Price Index data.

Options activity for TSLA on February 11, 2025, showed a bearish call option sweep expiring February 14, 2025, at a $350.00 strike price. The trade involved 252 contracts with a total trade price of $83.2K and a volume of 55,053 contracts.

Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau criticized new U.S. tariffs of 25% on steel and aluminum imports, effective March 4, as 'unacceptable.' The tariffs are expected to increase production costs for automotive companies including Tesla, Ford, and General Motors, with estimates suggesting a $2,000 price increase for new cars.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk confirmed the company is on track to launch autonomous ride-hailing in Austin, Texas, in June using Model Y and Model 3 vehicles equipped with Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. Musk also stated plans to expand the service to other American cities by the end of 2025. The dedicated Cybercab, which lacks steering wheels and pedals, is scheduled for production in 2026.

Former Tesla executive Jorge Milburn argued that Tesla could dominate the ride-hailing market through a saturation strategy and lower costs compared to Uber. However, fund manager Gary Black countered that Uber's 170 million monthly active users provide a significant cost advantage that Tesla's smaller network may struggle to match. Uber is currently partnering with Waymo for autonomous rides in Atlanta.

Tesla executive Max de Zegher stated that deploying a Supercharger stall currently costs between $40,000 and $45,000. The company aims to deploy its new V4 Superchargers with V4 cabinets for under $40,000 per stall, with pilot sites expected in Q3 2024. Tesla added over 11,000 stalls in 2024, bringing the global total to over 65,000.

Tesla VP of Vehicle Engineering Lars Moravy announced that the Performance variant of the refreshed Model Y will launch later in 2025. Production of the refreshed Model Y began recently across four global factories, with deliveries scheduled to start in March. Tesla reported 1.79 million global vehicle deliveries in 2024, down from 1.81 million in 2023.

Industry experts and investors are questioning Tesla's vision-only approach to autonomous driving as competitors like BYD and Huawei integrate LIDAR technology. Ross Gerber noted that all major competitors use LIDAR, while Gary Black suggested autonomous driving is becoming commoditized. Tesla's FSD system is currently under investigation by the NHTSA.

BYD has launched its 'Eyes of God' driver-assistance system for budget vehicles, including a $9,600 hatchback, in partnership with DeepSeek. This move challenges Tesla in the Chinese market, where Tesla faces regulatory delays for its FSD software. While Tesla charges $8,000 plus a $99 monthly subscription for FSD in the U.S., BYD is integrating its system as a standard feature.

Tesla has commenced production at its new Shanghai Megafactory less than nine months after construction began in May. The first Megapack, a battery unit capable of storing over 3.9 MWh, will be shipped to Australia. This facility is Tesla's first dedicated energy storage factory outside the U.S., complementing its Lathrop, California plant. Tesla's energy segment reported over $3 billion in Q4 revenue, with the company expecting at least 50% year-over-year growth in energy storage deployment for 2025.

Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. (CATL), a major battery supplier to Tesla, is preparing to file for a secondary listing in Hong Kong that could raise at least $5 billion. This move follows U.S. authorities adding CATL to a list of companies allegedly working with China's military on Jan. 7. The listing would be one of Hong Kong's largest in three years, as the company seeks to diversify funding amidst U.S.-China tensions.

On Monday, Tesla shares fell 3.01% to close at $350.73, following CEO Elon Musk's response to tax fraud allegations. The stock's 52-week range is $138.80 to $488.54. Other notable market movements included Super Micro Computer rising 17.56%, GameStop climbing 9.66%, McDonald's increasing 4.80%, and Monday.com soaring 26.46%.

U.S. Rep. Josh Gottheimer (D-N.J.) disclosed multiple stock transactions between Jan. 8 and Jan. 31, including selling Nvidia and Microsoft shares while purchasing Tesla stock in three transactions totaling $3,000 to $45,000. Gottheimer also bought Visa and Johnson & Johnson shares. The move into Tesla is noted for its potential connection to CEO Elon Musk's relationship with President Donald Trump.

Benchmark Equity Research initiated coverage on Tesla with a Buy rating and a $475 price target. Analyst Mickey Legg cited growth opportunities in autonomous vehicles, robotics, and energy storage. Key catalysts include the 2025 release of more affordable models, the June launch of unsupervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) in Austin, and the production ramp of the Optimus robot, with 10,000 units targeted for 2025 and external release in 2026. Tesla holds $36.5 billion in cash and investments.

The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) remains flat year-to-date despite a 45.17% one-year return. Tesla has been a significant drag on the fund, declining 10.53% YTD due to delivery and production challenges, high valuation, and competition. The ETF's equal-weight structure limits the impact of strong performers like Meta, while Nvidia, Apple, and Alphabet have also faced recent headwinds.

Tesla shares rebounded, jumping more than 4% on Wednesday after a 6.3% decline on Tuesday. The broader market recovered from initial losses following a hotter-than-expected January CPI report, which showed headline inflation at 3% year-over-year. Treasury yields surged as markets scaled back expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts to only one reduction by December 2025.
