Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) faces pressure from potential U.S. tariffs and threats to scrap the CHIPS and Science Act. While TSM stock rose 60% in the last 12 months, the company is considering building advanced packaging capacity in the U.S. TSM is a key chip supplier for Nvidia, Apple, Broadcom, and Tesla.

Oppenheimer analysts labeled Elon Musk's $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI a distraction from Tesla's (TSLA) challenges, including increased EV competition and political risks. TSLA stock has declined for five consecutive days, down 18.6% over the past month. Gary Black maintains a $380 price target on Tesla, while shares closed at $328.50 on Tuesday.

Adam Parker, CEO of Trivariate Research, advised investors to reduce exposure to the 'Magnificent Seven' stocks, including Tesla (TSLA), citing high valuations, over-ownership, and scrutiny over AI capital expenditures. Tesla has been the worst performer among the group year-to-date, down nearly 13%.

Tesla (TSLA) shares declined on Tuesday following news of a partnership between Chinese EV maker BYD and AI firm DeepSeek to develop self-driving technology. The broader market remains cautious ahead of January inflation data, with the Fed signaling no immediate interest rate changes.

Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) shares fell over 4% on Tuesday, hitting an eleven-week low, driven by weak January auto sales in China. This occurred as U.S. markets remained mixed following Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's comments indicating no immediate interest rate cuts.

Researcher Troy Teslike projects Tesla's Q1 deliveries will fall below 400,000 units, citing Model Y Juniper production limitations, seasonality, and brand sentiment. Tesla reported 1.79 million deliveries in 2024, down from 1.81 million in 2023. While CEO Elon Musk previously mentioned 20-30% growth for 2025, the company now states it expects the vehicle business to 'return to growth.' Refreshed Model Y production began recently, with deliveries expected in March.

Automakers like GM and Ford are lobbying for a three-year phase-out of EV tax credits rather than an abrupt end, citing risks to jobs and capital investments. This contrasts with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who has advocated for the elimination of EV subsidies. The industry faces potential impacts from new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada, as well as concerns over Chinese graphite.

Tesla has launched its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) technology in Mexico. The company advises drivers to remain cautious and ready to take control, noting that the system must adapt to local infrastructure and traffic patterns. While Tesla previously aimed to launch FSD in Europe and China in Q1 2025, CEO Elon Musk stated that regulatory and operational challenges have delayed these plans.

Lyft reported strong Q4 results with $1.6 billion in revenue and 44 million riders in 2024. The company plans to launch Mobileye-powered robotaxis in Dallas by 2026. In the autonomous vehicle space, Tesla aims to introduce its robotaxi service in Texas and California by 2025, while Uber has partnered with Waymo.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives remains bullish on Tesla's long-term robotics and AI vision despite current EV headwinds. Tesla's stock price dropped 17.29% between January 27 and February 11, 2025, closing at $328.50 on Tuesday. Ives contrasts this with Meta, where he sees AI initiatives potentially adding $100 per share.

Tesla shares fell 6.34% on Tuesday to close at $328.50, with an intraday range between $325.1 and $349.37. The decline followed reports that competitor BYD plans to incorporate self-driving technology into its vehicles. The stock's 52-week range is $138.8 to $488.54.

Tesla has filed trademarks for 'Robus' and 'Robobus,' potentially for its 20-person vehicle previously referred to as the Robovan. These filings cover electric buses, ridesharing, and peer-to-peer vehicle sharing. Robotaxis and the Robovan are key to Tesla's growth, with the company showcasing these technologies in October 2024. Tesla stock rose 4.3% to $350.85 on Thursday, down 7.4% year-to-date in 2025 but up over 90% in the last year.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who leads the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), has proposed deleting entire U.S. government agencies to achieve radical spending cuts. Musk also announced that X's AI chatbot, Grok 3, is expected in approximately two weeks. Tesla stock has declined over 16% in the past month since President Trump's inauguration and Musk's appointment to DOGE.

Apple is reportedly exploring humanoid and non-humanoid robots for its smart home ecosystem, according to analyst Ming-Chi Kuo. These projects are in the early proof-of-concept stage, with mass production unlikely before 2028. This move is seen as competition for Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot.

Following a January CPI print of 3% year-over-year, U.S. stock futures were mixed on Thursday. Tesla rebounded over 2% on Wednesday after a five-day losing streak, following a broader market decline where the S&P 500 fell 0.27% and the Dow fell 0.50%.

Tesla stock rose 2.2% in pre-market trading following bullish analyst notes. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained a $550 price target, while Benchmark Equity analyst Mickey Legg set a $475 target, citing growth in autonomous vehicles, robotics, and energy. President Donald Trump nominated Jonathan Morrison, an Apple employee and former NHTSA chief counsel, to lead the NHTSA. The agency is currently investigating Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) software, including a probe into 2.6 million vehicles regarding the 'Actually Smart Summon' feature and a separate investigation into 2.4 million vehicles following four crashes in low-visibility conditions. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy committed to allowing these investigations to proceed.

Elon Musk denied knowledge of a potential $400 million government contract for Tesla armored vehicles. The U.S. State Department's 2025 procurement forecast previously listed such a purchase but removed the company's name following conflict of interest concerns regarding Musk's role in the Department of Government Efficiency. Tesla reported a 6% drop in 2024 automotive revenue to $77.07 billion.

Tesla increased discounts on 2024 inventory Cybertrucks to $6,000 for the foundation series and $5,100 for the non-foundation series. In January, discounts were capped at $2,000. The Cybertruck was Tesla's third best-selling vehicle in the U.S. in 2024, with 38,965 units sold.

Short-seller Jim Chanos questioned a potential $400 million government contract for armored Cybertrucks, linking it to Elon Musk's leadership of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). Tesla's stock has declined 20.64% since January 21. Analyst Dan Ives maintains a $550 price target, dismissing concerns that Musk's DOGE role causes significant brand deterioration.

Tesla is testing 'DyeDefender' blue dye bombs and 'Property of Tesla' cable engravings at its New York factory to combat Supercharger cable theft and vandalism. While current vehicles use cables, Tesla's upcoming Cybercab, expected to enter production in 2026, will utilize inductive charging.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintains a $550 price target for Tesla, arguing that concerns over Elon Musk's time commitment to the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) are overstated. Conversely, Miller Tabak strategist Matt Maley expressed concern that Musk's focus on DOGE could delay Tesla's product development. Tesla's stock has fallen 20.64% since January 21, closing at $336.51 on Wednesday.

A bearish put option sweep was recorded for Tesla (TSLA) expiring on February 21, 2025, with a $350.00 strike price. The transaction involved 72 contracts split across 5 trades, totaling $71.8K in premium, with 24,349 contracts traded today.

Defiance ETFs launched the Battleshares TSLA vs. F ETF (NYSE: ELON), an actively managed fund using a long/short strategy. The ETF takes a +200% leveraged long position in Tesla and a -100% leveraged short position in Ford to capitalize on the competitive dynamics between the two automakers.
The article reports on defined-risk options trades, noting a 115% profit on a Tesla (TSLA) call option bought at $1.91 on February 11, 2025, and sold at $4.10 on February 13, 2025.

UBS reported that Tesla's January global market share declined, with European sales falling 51% year-over-year and Chinese retail sales dropping 15%. While U.S. EV sales rose 25%, UBS labeled January a 'very poor month' for Tesla due to rising competition from brands like Xpeng, which saw a 251% sales surge. The report also noted potential risks from U.S. auto tariffs and highlighted Tesla's ongoing challenges in China, including security-related bans and competition from BYD and Huawei.

Tesla shares have fallen over 25% from their December 17 peak of $479.86. Fundstrat's Mark Newton warns the stock has not bottomed and could drop another 12% in the next two weeks. While CEO Elon Musk announced robotaxi deployments in Austin for June, analysts like Chris McNally see limited near-term catalysts. Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi expressed interest in a partnership to deploy Tesla's autonomous vehicles on the Uber platform, stating he prefers not to compete with Tesla. Uber previously exited the self-driving car business in 2020. Tesla CEO Elon Musk plans to begin robotaxi ride-hailing operations in Austin in June, envisioning a fleet model where both Tesla and individual owners contribute vehicles.

U.S. stocks rose on Thursday, with the Dow gaining over 300 points as the Fear & Greed Index moved to 'Neutral.' The Producer Price Index rose 3.5% year-over-year in January, while initial jobless claims fell to 213,000. Tesla shares gained 5.8% during the session, supported by positive analyst commentary and news regarding new EV taxes for road repairs.

U.S. stock futures were flat on Friday following Thursday's gains, where the Dow rose 0.77% and the Nasdaq gained 1.5% behind rallies in Nvidia and Tesla. January PPI rose 3.5% annually. Wells Fargo strategist Scott Wren expressed caution regarding a third consecutive year of 20%+ S&P 500 gains, projecting a 12% gain for the year. Investors are monitoring upcoming economic data, including retail sales and industrial production.

Bridgewater Associates' Karen Karniol-Tambour challenged concerns that 'Magnificent Seven' stocks, including Tesla, are overvalued. She argued that these companies are fairly priced if they sustain a 20% earnings growth rate, noting they only need 14% annual growth to justify their current premiums. This contrasts with other analysts, such as Adam Parker of Trivariate Research, who view the tech giants as overvalued and risky.

Tesla shares rose 5.77% to close at $355.94, with an intraday high of $358.69 and a low of $342.85. The stock remains between its 52-week high of $488.54 and low of $138.80. A meeting between Elon Musk and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi regarding potential collaborations and tariff discussions has sparked interest in Tesla's potential entry into the Indian market.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Tesla and SpaceX CEO Elon Musk in Washington, D.C. Discussions reportedly covered Tesla's potential entry into India, Starlink's licensing application, and potential SpaceX-ISRO collaborations. Musk has advocated for reduced tariffs on Tesla vehicles to export low-cost models to India. Tesla's stock closed at $355.94, up 5.77%, and climbed 0.53% in after-hours trading to $357.81.

Activist investor Dan Loeb's Third Point LLC increased its stake in Tesla by 25% (100,000 shares) during the fourth quarter of 2024. The fund also increased holdings in Meta Platforms and Flutter Entertainment, while trimming positions in Microsoft and Amazon and selling its entire Apple stake.

President Donald Trump suggested potential tariffs on automobiles could be implemented as early as April 2. This follows recent trade actions including tariffs on Chinese imports and steel/aluminum. Tesla rivals Ford and General Motors have been advocating for a gradual phase-out of electric vehicle tax credits, which Trump canceled last month.

Meta is reportedly planning a significant investment into AI-powered humanoid robots, forming a new team within its Reality Labs division led by former GM Cruise CEO Marc Whitten. Meta is discussing the initiative with robotics firms like Unitree Robotics and Figure AI. This follows Tesla's announced plans to produce thousands of Optimus humanoid robots for its factories, which Musk projects could generate over $10 trillion in long-term revenue.

Apple and Meta are reportedly developing AI-powered humanoid robots, potentially competing with Tesla's Optimus. Meta has established a new unit within its Reality Labs division to develop software platforms for humanoid technology. Apple is exploring humanoid robotics through its advanced AI research teams. Google's DeepMind is also investigating robotics technology.

Musician Sheryl Crow announced she is parting ways with her Tesla vehicle, citing disapproval of CEO Elon Musk's political ties and his role leading the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) under the Trump administration.

Tesla shares have dropped over 25% from their December peak, with analyst Mark Newton predicting a potential further 12% decline in the next one to two weeks. Meanwhile, Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi expressed interest in a partnership to deploy Tesla's autonomous vehicles on Uber's platform. Additionally, President Donald Trump has hinted at potential auto tariffs starting April 2, and Elon Musk's Boring Company plans to build a 'Dubai Loop' capable of transporting 20,000 passengers per hour.

The Treasury Department is auditing security controls for federal payment systems following concerns regarding access granted to Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). Separately, President Donald Trump has nominated Jonathan Morrison, an Apple employee and former NHTSA chief counsel, to lead the U.S. auto safety regulator, which oversees Tesla. Additionally, short-seller Jim Chanos has questioned a potential $400 million, five-year government contract for Tesla's armored Cybertrucks.

A report indicates that Elon Musk is supporting right-wing political movements and policies in at least 18 countries, focusing on reducing immigration and curbing business regulation. Musk has used his platform X to engage with political figures and protests globally, including in Brazil, Ireland, India, and Turkey. While some right-wing politicians benefit from his association, leaders like French President Emmanuel Macron have criticized his influence, and political scientists note the growing power of tech billionaires in shaping global politics.

Tesla's California sales dropped to 26.1% of its total U.S. sales in Q4 2024, the lowest level in four years, down from 27.9% in Q4 2023. Researcher Troy Teslike suggests this decline may be linked to CEO Elon Musk's political actions. This trend aligns with broader challenges, including a 12% drop in California sales for 2024 and a 13% decline in European registrations, contributing to Tesla's first annual delivery decline.

Elon Musk referenced the Bill Clinton-era reduction of the federal workforce to defend current downsizing efforts amid protests at Tesla showrooms in New York, Seattle, and California. The controversy has impacted the brand, with Ross Gerber planning to sell his Cybertruck due to public hostility. Additionally, Tesla's German sales reportedly fell 60% year-over-year in January. Tesla shares closed at $355.84 on Friday, marking a 14.01% decline over the past month.

BYD plans to start demonstration use of all-solid-state batteries by 2027, with broader adoption after 2030. The company has been developing this technology since 2013 and is currently conducting trial production of cells with 20 Ah and 60 Ah capacities. BYD CTO Sun Huajun noted that sulfide electrolytes are being used, and while costs are currently high due to low volume, they are expected to become comparable to liquid ternary batteries as adoption grows. These batteries are intended primarily for high-end vehicle models.

Ray Dalio's Bridgewater Associates trimmed its holdings in six 'Magnificent 7' stocks during Q4 2024, including Apple (-40%), Amazon (-35%), Nvidia (-26%), Microsoft (-23%), Meta (-23%), and Alphabet (-17%). Conversely, the fund added 153,589 shares of Tesla, valued at $62.025 million, marking its first position in the company since Q4 2021. Dalio expressed concerns regarding high valuations of tech stocks, emphasizing a preference for value and productivity-driven investments.

Goldman Sachs raised its MSCI China Index target from 75 to 85, forecasting a 16% upside and potential $200 billion inflow into Chinese stocks over the next 12 months. Analysts predict AI adoption, including models like DeepSeek, could boost Chinese companies' EPS by 2.5% annually over the next decade. The report contrasts the valuation of the 'Magnificent Seven' (average P/E of 41.4) with the Hang Seng Tech Index (average P/E of 20.5).

Ross Gerber, CEO of Gerber Kawasaki, announced plans to sell his Cybertruck due to public hostility toward the Tesla brand. This follows protests at Tesla showrooms in cities like New York and Seattle regarding Elon Musk's role in the Department of Government Efficiency. Additionally, singer Sheryl Crow documented selling her Tesla, and reports indicate a 60% year-over-year drop in German Tesla sales in January. Tesla shares fell 14.01% over the past month.

Viking Global Investors exited its entire positions in Apple (4.86 million shares) and Tesla (over 436,000 shares) during Q4 2024. The firm also trimmed its Nvidia holdings by 10.3%. The move reflects a broader sector shift, with Viking reducing its Information Technology exposure from 19.06% to 17.96% while increasing its Financials allocation from 25.58% to 28.45%.

Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates expressed optimism in an interview that Elon Musk will reconsider his plans to slash USAID programs. Musk, who leads the Department of Government Efficiency, has publicly criticized USAID as wasteful. Gates defended the agency's work in famine relief and HIV medication distribution, stating it is something taxpayers should be proud of.

OpenAI is considering granting special voting rights to its non-profit board to defend against unsolicited takeover bids, including a recent $97.4 billion offer from Elon Musk which the board rejected. Analysts at Oppenheimer suggested that Musk's acquisition attempt serves as a diversion from his leadership at Tesla. The proposed governance changes would allow the board to overrule major investors like Microsoft and SoftBank.

On February 14, 2025, a U.S. District Court Judge denied Tesla's request for a temporary restraining order against Matthews International regarding its dry battery electrode (DBE) technology. This follows an early February 2025 arbitration win for Matthews. Tesla subsequently filed a new lawsuit in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California. Matthews, which holds U.S. Patent No. 12,136,727, claims the lawsuit is meritless. Matthews has been a supplier to Tesla since 2019.

Tesla is offering free XPEL colored vinyl wraps for Foundation Series Cybertruck buyers who take delivery by March 31, 2025. The Foundation Series, a limited edition with laser-etched badges, starts at $99,990, while the regular Cybertruck starts at $79,990. Tesla is also offering discounts of up to $6,000 on the Foundation Series to clear inventory.

Fund manager Gary Black advised Tesla investors to analyze the stock objectively rather than emotionally. He warned against relying on management's production forecasts instead of demand. Black remains optimistic about Tesla, citing the potential for a new, cheaper hatchback model priced between $25,000 and $30,000 to expand the company's total addressable market, provided it features a new form factor.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk, serving on the Department of Government Efficiency, highlighted statistics showing millions of Americans over 100 years old listed as alive in Social Security Administration (SSA) records. Analysts noted the stock was also supported by plans for a June robotaxi launch and production of affordable EVs in the first half of the year. Despite the rally, Tesla stock is down 34% year-to-date.

The company stated that exaggerated or unfounded criticism has incited protests and violence against its operations. Musk attributed these protests to those wishing to maintain government waste and fraud. The filing follows reports of vandalism against Tesla properties and rising insurance costs for owners.

This follows a report, which Musk and the board denied, claiming the company was seeking a new CEO. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives expects Musk to remain CEO for at least five more years.

Tesla has outperformed the market over the past 10 years with an average annual return of 33.19%. An investment of $100 in Tesla stock 10 years ago would be worth $1,745.65 today, based on a current price of $283.81. The company currently holds a market capitalization of $914.14 billion.

The USPTO rejected Tesla's trademark application for 'Robotaxi,' labeling it 'merely descriptive' and generic. Tesla's 'Cybercab' trademark application was also halted due to similar filings by other companies. Tesla has three months to respond to the rejection. The company has also filed for 'Robobus' and 'Robus' trademarks, which remain under review. Tesla stock is down 24.2% year-to-date in 2025.

Tesla shares rose 4.4% to $288.44 on Thursday, driven by market optimism following a U.S.-U.K. trade deal. Countries facing U.S. tariffs are fast-tracking approvals for Elon Musk's Starlink to improve trade relations with the Trump administration. Cathie Wood of ARK Invest reiterated her bullish stance on Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA), maintaining a price target of $2,600 per share over the next five years. This target excludes potential revenue from humanoid robots, which she values as a $26 trillion opportunity.

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) shares rose 7.1% to $305.13 on Friday. The company is finalizing plans to launch operations in India, though Prashanth Menon, the Country Head for India, has reportedly resigned after nine years with the company.

Tesla employee Matthew Labrot was fired after creating a website, 'Tesla Employees Against Elon,' which criticized CEO Elon Musk's political views and called for his replacement. Labrot also spray-painted his Cybertruck with anti-Musk messages. Lucid Group is offering discounts of up to $20,000 on its Air sedan in May, including a $4,000 bonus for customers trading in a Tesla vehicle. The offer is valid for purchases made before May 31, 2025.

Investor Gary Black reaffirmed that Tesla is well-positioned to capitalize on EV adoption and autonomous driving, though he noted the upcoming Austin robotaxi launch carries 'asymmetrical risk/return.' Tesla plans to launch robotaxis in June, with CEO Elon Musk confirming 10-20 Model Y units for the event. The company's trademark applications for 'Robotaxi' and 'Cybercab' were recently refused by the USPTO.

Prashanth Menon, Tesla's Country Head for India, has resigned after nine years. Texas lawmakers have passed Senate Bill 29 and Senate Bill 1057, which aim to reduce small shareholder influence and make it harder to sue corporations. These bills, supported by companies like Tesla, are seen as a move to shield businesses from legal challenges similar to those faced in Delaware, such as the court decision that struck down Elon Musk's pay package.

XPeng cofounder He Xiaopeng reported that 50% of new cars sold in China last year were new energy vehicles (NEVs), with 55.7% of those featuring advanced automated driving assist systems. In contrast, the U.S. and Europe see NEV sales at 11% and 23% respectively, with Tesla having advanced features in about 25% of its vehicles. XPeng aims for over half of its sales to come from overseas within the next decade.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman reached out to Elon Musk on X on May 10, 2025, suggesting they move past their feud to focus on the importance of artificial general intelligence (AGI). This follows Musk's recent criticism of OpenAI's direction and Altman's involvement in the $500 billion Project Stargate for AI infrastructure.

Tesla has over 10,000 Cybertrucks in inventory and has discontinued the range extender battery pack, which was intended to provide over 500 miles of range. Future Fund LLC's Gary Black criticized Tesla for poor marketing and communication.

Ives noted that Tesla CEO Elon Musk is focused on the company's autonomy strategy ahead of a June robotaxi launch.

Bill Gates criticized Tesla CEO Elon Musk's goal of cutting $2 trillion from the U.S. government budget, specifically targeting the dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID). The article details a long-standing rivalry between the two, including Musk's claims that Gates held a significant short position against Tesla stock, which Gates has neither confirmed nor denied.

Benzinga reported unusual options activity for TSLA on June 24, 2025, noting a bearish call option sweep expiring June 27, 2025. 58 contracts were traded at a $350.00 strike price, totaling $26.5K in premiums across 16 trades.

Tesla options activity shows 175 uncommon trades with a split sentiment (34% bullish, 50% bearish). Major movers focus on a $320-$600 price band. Five analysts provide a consensus target of $304.0, with ratings ranging from Sell (Guggenheim $175, UBS $215) to Overweight (Morgan Stanley $410, Piper Sandler $400) and Neutral (Baird $320). TSLA is trading at $343.16, down 1.58%.

Fidelity portfolio manager George Efstathopoulos favors Chinese tech stocks over U.S. 'Magnificent Seven' due to innovation and valuation. Tesla trades at a forward P/E of 178, while Chinese EV competitor BYD trades at 19. Tesla has committed $8 billion to EV and AI development this year.

ARK Invest predicts humanoid robot adoption could unlock $250 billion in GDP. Tesla, Nvidia, and AMD are identified as key drivers. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang suggests Tesla's Optimus could achieve mass adoption, while Elon Musk claims Optimus manufacturing costs half that of a Tesla EV.

Tesla's Chinese sales for the week of June 16-22 saw 13,800 new insured registrations, an 11% decline from the previous week and 21% year-on-year. Year-to-date sales are down 7.6%. Tesla recently invested $550 million in a Shanghai grid-scale energy storage facility.

Tesla faces a wrongful death lawsuit in New Jersey regarding a September 14, 2024, Model S crash that killed three people. The plaintiffs allege the vehicle's design is defective and dangerous, questioning the safety of Tesla's ADAS technology.

The NHTSA is investigating potential traffic law violations by Tesla's Robotaxis during a limited launch event in Austin. Social media footage shows vehicles speeding and making improper lane maneuvers. Tesla remains under separate regulatory scrutiny regarding its Full Self-Driving (FSD) performance in poor weather.

Tesla is attempting to keep its responses to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) regarding FSD technology private, citing federal law and concerns that competitors could misuse the information. The NHTSA investigation remains open, specifically regarding FSD performance in poor weather. This comes as Tesla launches its Robotaxi in Austin, Texas, amid scrutiny following a recent incident where a Model 3 on FSD was struck by a train.

Ark Invest CEO Cathie Wood predicts that electric vehicles will become more economical than gasoline-powered cars within three years, using China's rapid EV growth as a model. BloombergNEF data estimates China's EV sales will reach 18 million units by 2026. Meanwhile, Tesla CEO Elon Musk has noted the competitive challenge posed by rivals selling EVs at negative gross margins, and new entrants like the Jeff Bezos-backed Slate Auto are planning affordable EV models for late 2026.

Tesla shares rose 8.23% to close at $348.68 on Monday, following the launch of its Robotaxi in Austin, Texas. CEO Elon Musk stated that the company's self-driving technology can be deployed anywhere it is approved. The stock's 52-week range is $182 to $488.54.

The article discusses the legacy of FedEx founder Fred Smith, who died on June 21 at age 80. It highlights how Smith turned a college paper that received a 'C' grade into a $54 billion logistics giant. The text draws parallels between Smith's path and other successful figures who deviated from traditional academic tracks, including Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who left a Stanford Ph.D. program after two days to pursue business ventures.

Tesla shares rose 9.3% to $352.11 on Monday following the launch of its Robotaxi in Austin, Texas, where a limited number of Model Y units were deployed. The company stated that its approach to autonomy allows for scalability, enabling self-driving deployment wherever approved.

BYD has surpassed Tesla in European EV registrations, driven by a rapid product offensive including the affordable Dolphin Surf EV and luxury Denza Yangwang models. BYD plans to launch a new manufacturing plant in Hungary by the end of 2025. S&P Global Mobility projects BYD's European sales could reach 186,000 this year and potentially 400,000 by 2029.
Tesla shares fell 4.5% on Wednesday following a report that the company's new car sales in Europe declined 27% year-over-year.

Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney maintained a Neutral rating on Tesla with a $285 price target. Tesla launched robotaxi operations in Austin on June 22 with 10-20 Model Y vehicles, but Delaney notes scaling will be slow due to limited operating areas, navigation issues, and a restricted user base compared to Waymo. He projects 2025 revenue of $89.5 billion and EPS of $1.10.

Tesla shares fell 4.14% to $326.40 after reporting a 27.9% year-over-year decline in European vehicle sales, marking five consecutive months of drops. Tesla's European market share fell to 1.2% from 1.8% last year. Analysts remain divided: Wedbush maintains an Outperform rating with a $500 target, while UBS and Guggenheim maintain Sell ratings with targets of $215 and $175, respectively.

Institutional investors and hedge funds significantly increased positions in major tech stocks during Q1, including $10.12 billion in Tesla. ETFs like IVV, VOO, and SPY have seen massive inflows, with VOO garnering $82 billion in inflows as of May 18. The trend is driven by optimism regarding AI, cloud infrastructure demand, and earnings resilience.

Tenev highlighted that Robinhood is expanding into prediction markets and seeking regulatory clarity for crypto in the U.S., noting that Europe currently offers a more favorable regulatory environment for crypto innovation.

Tesla, Uber, and Waymo are pursuing different autonomous vehicle strategies. Tesla utilizes vertical integration to control production and software, recently launching a limited robotaxi service in Austin with over 10 Model Ys. Waymo operates its own fleet but partners with Uber to scale, while Uber functions as a platform connecting riders to autonomous fleets without manufacturing its own vehicles.

ARK Invest projects Tesla stock will reach $2,600 by 2030, representing an $8.19 trillion market cap, while valuing SpaceX at $2.5 trillion. Despite SpaceX's growth potential through Starlink, which ARK expects to capture 15% of global communications spending, Tesla remains ARK's top pick due to its broader innovation stack including EVs, Optimus robots, and energy storage.

BYD has reduced production shifts at four Chinese factories and halted new production lines due to profitability concerns and rising inventory. Additionally, Tesla is facing a wrongful death lawsuit involving a 2024 Model S crash and has invoked federal law to keep FSD-related information confidential.

Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.) announced plans to reprogram his debt clock badge to track the time since Donald Trump last tweeted attacks against him. The feud follows Massie's opposition to Trump's tax legislation, which Tesla CEO Elon Musk also criticized as a 'disgusting abomination.'

Tesla's European sales fell for the fifth consecutive month in May, declining 27.9% year-on-year to 13,863 units, with market share dropping to 1.3%. Year-to-date sales in Europe are down 37.1%. While sales grew in Norway, they declined by nearly 11% in China.

Uber shares neared an all-time high following the expansion of its driverless ride-hailing partnership with Waymo to Atlanta. Gary Black of Future Fund LLC described the partnership as 'low-risk' while contrasting it with Tesla's Robotaxi service, which he noted still operates in 'supervised autonomy mode.'
Tesla shares fell 2.35% to close at $340.47 on Tuesday. The decline follows reports of a decrease in Chinese sales during the third week of June, despite growth in May.

Xiaomi launched its YU7 electric SUV in China at a starting price of 253,500 yuan, undercutting Tesla's Model Y. Xiaomi reported over 200,000 pre-orders within three minutes. The YU7 features a 760-kilometer range and Nvidia's Thor chip.

Tesla vice president Omead Afshar was reportedly fired by Elon Musk days after publicly praising the company's robotaxi push. Afshar, who oversaw sales and manufacturing in North America and Europe, joins other recent departures including Milan Kovac and Jenna Ferrua. Tesla shares rose 2.2% to $307.34 on Wednesday following Q2 2025 results showing 410,244 vehicles produced and 384,122 delivered, with 373,728 being Model 3/Y units. The company also deployed 9.6 GWh of energy storage. Musk will personally oversee U.S. and European sales operations following the departure of Omead Afshar. Analyst ratings remain divided: Wedbush maintains an Outperform rating ($500 target), while Guggenheim and UBS maintain Sell ratings ($175 and $215 targets, respectively).

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that the feud between Elon Musk and President Donald Trump is unlikely to affect the passage of a debt-reduction bill, which recently passed the Senate via a tie-breaking vote from Vice President JD Vance. Bessent expressed confidence that the bill, which includes tax cuts and spending adjustments, will move forward under Trump's leadership. Analysts have warned that the Musk-Trump rift could complicate Tesla's push for autonomous vehicle regulation.

Bank of America reports that autonomous vehicles (AVs) have reached a 'ChatGPT moment' due to AI advancements. The total addressable market is estimated at $1.2 trillion by 2040, covering robotaxis, trucks, and industrial vehicles. As of April 2025, robotaxis have completed 186 million commercial miles across seven cities. The report identifies 28 key stocks in the sector, including Caterpillar, Deere, Uber, and Nvidia, but notably excludes Tesla.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk is receiving support from Chinese social media users on Weibo following his feud with President Donald Trump over the 'One Big, Beautiful Bill,' which Musk claims increases the U.S. debt ceiling by $5 trillion. Trump suggested Musk would have to 'close up shop' without government subsidies. Musk has threatened to form an 'America Party' if the spending bill passes.

Venture capitalist Vinod Khosla invested in OpenAI in 2018 after Elon Musk failed to meet his funding commitments. Khosla cited concerns over Google's slow progress and Baidu's talent acquisition as factors in his decision. OpenAI is now valued at $300 billion following a $40 billion funding round. Musk, a co-founder who left in 2018, is currently pursuing legal challenges against the company's transition to a for-profit structure.

Roth Capital Partners analyst Craig Irwin warned that Elon Musk's feud with President Trump could negatively impact Tesla's autonomous vehicle regulatory goals. Despite Tesla shares falling 5.34% to $300.71 on Tuesday amid the political tension, Irwin remains bullish on the stock, citing potential Level 4 autonomy and Optimus robot updates. Trump recently criticized Musk's reliance on government subsidies.

BYD reported its best sales month of 2025 in June, selling 377,628 units, an 11% year-over-year increase. EVs accounted for 54.8% of sales, growing 42.5%. Exports surged 234% to 90,049 units compared to June 2024. The company is expanding its global footprint, including a production facility in Hungary and the release of the Dolphin Surf EV in Europe for $26,000.

Fundstrat's Tom Lee argued that Tesla's success is driven by engineering talent, manufacturing expertise, AI, and data, rather than government subsidies. This follows President Trump's claim that Tesla would fail without subsidies. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintains a bullish outlook on Tesla, forecasting a $2 trillion valuation, while Tesla shares closed down 5.34% at $300.71 on Tuesday.

Following the exit of aide Omead Afshar, Elon Musk will personally oversee Tesla sales in the U.S. and Europe. Executive Tom Zhu will continue to manage sales in Asia while taking over global production operations. Tesla is facing sales declines in several regions, including Canada and parts of Scandinavia, but plans to introduce more affordable vehicle models in 2025.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk praised President Donald Trump for his role in facilitating a Gaza ceasefire, announced July 2 following 60-day negotiations. Despite this, Musk has threatened to launch an 'America Party' over Trump's spending bill. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives predicts weak Tesla performance due to the fallout between Musk and Trump, noting Tesla shares fell 5.34% on Tuesday after Trump suggested Musk would 'close up shop' without government subsidies.

Tesla confirmed in its 2024 impact report that it will launch more affordable vehicle models in 2025 to accelerate EV adoption. This announcement follows declining sales in markets like Sweden, Denmark, and Canada, which some experts attribute to CEO Elon Musk's political associations. Investor Gary Black noted that the departure of Musk's aide, Omead Afshar, reflects poor sales figures.

A lawyer for Elon Musk's X, K.G. Raghavan, sparked a dispute with the Indian government by calling a government content takedown portal a 'censorship portal' and stating that every 'Tom, Dick and Harry' official could issue removal orders. The Indian government maintains the portal is for due diligence. This conflict occurs as Tesla prepares for an India debut, with a Model Y already spotted testing in Mumbai.

Investor Gary Black predicts Tesla's Q2 deliveries will miss market estimates, forecasting 370,000 units (a 17% YoY decline) compared to the 385,000 estimate. Black suggests the focus remains on the scalability of the Robotaxi, noting recent tests in Austin involved a safety driver and some disengagements. He also cited the departure of Omead Afshar as confirmation of poor Q2 performance.

Cryptocurrencies and tech stocks fell Tuesday following the Senate's passage of President Donald Trump's tax-and-spending bill. Tesla shares dropped 5.34% as part of the tech sell-off. Analyst Ali Martinez identified a 'rare' quarterly sell signal for Bitcoin via the TD Sequential indicator, warning of a potential drop below $40,000.

Tesla shares fell 5.34% on Tuesday to close at $300.71, with an intraday range of $293.21 to $305.89. The decline is attributed to disagreements between CEO Elon Musk and President Donald Trump, alongside reports of falling vehicle registrations in Sweden and Denmark during June.

Cathie Wood's Ark Invest purchased 56,368 Tesla shares across its ARKK and ARKW funds for approximately $16.95 million on Tuesday, despite a share price decline linked to Musk's feud with Donald Trump. Ark remains bullish on Tesla, which is its largest holding at 9.7% of its portfolio. Simultaneously, Ark sold 9,116 Coinbase shares valued at $3.06 million amid the exchange's ongoing legal challenges.

The U.S. Senate passed a tax-and-spending bill in a 51-50 vote, with Vice President JD Vance breaking the tie. The legislation, which adds $3.3 trillion to the national debt and raises the debt ceiling by $5 trillion, now moves to the House. It includes tax cuts, spending changes, and the repeal of green-energy incentives. Tesla CEO Elon Musk expressed displeasure with the bill and threatened to launch an 'America Party' if it passes.

In June, U.S. ETF inflows reached $102.7 billion, with investors showing high conviction in leveraged, single-stock ETFs tied to Tesla, Nvidia, and Palantir. The Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSL) saw $134.9 million in inflows in June and leads year-to-date flows with $310.8 million. Overall, equity flows rose 29% to $57 billion, signaling a return to risk-on sentiment in tech and AI-related sectors.

A Benzinga poll conducted from July 1 to July 3, 2025, surveyed 102 adults on their preferred business mentor. Tesla CEO Elon Musk ranked first with 36% of the vote, followed by Mark Cuban with 30%. The poll also included Jensen Huang (23%), Mark Zuckerberg (8%), and Jeff Bezos (3%). Musk and Cuban are noted for their entrepreneurial success, hands-on management, and public profiles.

Tesla's Cybertruck demand appears to be stalling, with annualized deliveries estimated at 20,000, significantly below the original 250,000 annual production goal. In the second quarter of 2025, Tesla delivered 10,394 vehicles in its 'Other' category, which includes the Cybertruck, Model S, Model X, and Semi. Tesla stock is down 16.7% year-to-date, trading at $315.36 as of Thursday.

Options activity in the Consumer Discretionary sector shows a bearish call option sweep for Tesla (TSLA). Specifically, 200 call contracts with a $320.00 strike price expiring on July 11, 2025, were traded for a total of $148.0K. The trade was split into 19 separate transactions, with 18,921 contracts traded today.

Nebius Group (NASDAQ: NBIS), an AI infrastructure startup backed by Nvidia and Tesla, is focusing on autonomous mobility through its U.S. subsidiary, Avride Inc. Avride, a 2020 spin-off from Yandex, operates robotaxis and delivery bots for Uber in Austin, Dallas, and Jersey City using retrofitted Hyundai Ioniq 5s. In March 2025, Nebius partnered with Hyundai for commercial robotaxi development. The company aims to be a sovereign AI operating system for autonomous mobility, with manufacturing in Taiwan.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk's net worth has declined by $71.2 billion year-to-date in 2025, a drop larger than the combined losses of the next seven billionaire decliners. Tesla stock is down 16.7% YTD in 2025, trading over $400 at the start of the year before facing volatility and concerns over Musk's relationship with President Trump. Musk currently has an estimated net worth of $361 billion.

Errol Musk, father of Tesla CEO Elon Musk, expressed confusion regarding the ongoing feud between his son and President Donald Trump. He advised Elon to stay silent and focus on his businesses, while also supporting Trump's spending bill, stating the U.S. needs rebuilding. Errol urged both parties to 'talk it out' to resolve their tensions.

Xiaomi CEO Lei Jun announced plans to sell electric vehicles internationally starting in 2027. The company is currently focused on domestic deliveries of its SU7 sedan and YU7 SUV in China. The YU7 SUV, which competes with the Tesla Model Y, received nearly 300,000 orders in under an hour. Tesla recently reported over 61,000 vehicle deliveries in China during June.

Tesla has reportedly halted production of its Optimus humanoid robot to undergo hardware and software redesigns. The company had produced over 1,000 units, falling short of Elon Musk's 5,000-unit target. Suppliers were told it would take at least two months to determine new production plans. Issues cited include overheating motors and short battery life. Ashok Elluswamy has taken over the Optimus line.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives reiterated an 'Outperform' rating and $500 price target for Tesla (TSLA) following Q2 deliveries of 384,122 units, which beat street estimates of approximately 365,000. Ives noted a rebound in Chinese sales and expects growth to accelerate in the second half of 2025. He warned that Musk's political feud with the Trump administration could negatively impact the company's government relations.

Tesla reported 61,000 vehicle sales in China during June, a 3.7% year-over-year increase and a 59% jump compared to May. This surge contrasts with declining sales in Sweden, Denmark, and Canada. The results contributed to Tesla's total Q2 deliveries of over 384,000 units, driven primarily by the Model 3 and Model Y.

Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg was reportedly asked to leave an Oval Office meeting regarding the F-47 stealth fighter jet due to security clearance concerns, though a Trump official stated the situation was mischaracterized and Zuckerberg was merely waiting for a scheduled meeting with the President. The report notes Meta's potential $60 billion investment in the U.S. by the end of 2025 and mentions Tesla CEO Elon Musk's influence on Meta's internal culture.

The Libertarian National Committee, led by Steven Nekhaila, has invited Elon Musk to join their party, arguing that creating a new third party would be a mistake. Nekhaila highlighted the party's existing ballot access in almost every state as a key advantage. Musk, who has invested over $250 million in the 2024 election through his America PAC, has not responded. Musk previously hinted at forming an 'America Party' following his criticism of a GOP spending bill. No Labels leader Dan Webb has also expressed interest in engaging with Musk.

Tesla stock rose 5% after the company reported Q2 2025 deliveries of 384,122 vehicles, which was in line with Wall Street estimates and better than the feared 355,000-360,000 range. Investor Gary Black noted the performance was driven by refreshed Model Y deliveries and strong China sales. The company faces declining sales in Sweden, Norway, and Canada, with some reports suggesting Canadian sales are near zero. Musk is currently overseeing U.S. and European sales following the departure of Omead Afshar.

Tesla stock climbed 4.97% to close at $315.65 on Wednesday, with an intraday high of $316.83. The company, led by Elon Musk, confirmed plans to introduce more affordable models to its lineup amid declining sales. The stock's 52-week range is between $182 and $488.54.

Unusual options activity for Tesla (TSLA) was observed on July 2, 2025, involving a bearish call option sweep expiring on July 11, 2025. The trade involved 200 contracts at a $320.00 strike price, split across 19 trades. The total cost was $148,000, with a price of $740 per contract. There were 10,560 open contracts at this strike prior to the activity, with 18,921 contracts traded during the session.

Tesla is currently focused on its Robotaxi rollout in Austin, which has driven stock momentum, though the company faces regulatory scrutiny from the NHTSA regarding safety concerns and traffic violations by its self-driving cars. Technically, the stock has dipped below most moving averages, finding support at the 200-day simple moving average. The RSI is neutral at 47.59, and the MACD is negative, suggesting caution for investors.

Ford CEO Jim Farley criticized Tesla's camera-only autonomous driving approach, favoring the lidar-based system used by Alphabet's Waymo for safety and consumer trust. Tesla is currently valued at a $1.02 trillion market cap with a forward P/E of 163.9x, significantly higher than Alphabet (19.1x) and Ford (6.7x). Farley noted that Ford has shifted its focus from Level 4 autonomy to 'eyes-off' highway driving, questioning the logic of Tesla's current valuation based on a robotaxi future.

Tesla stock fell 0.73% this week, closing at $315.35 on Thursday, amid a 'war of words' between Elon Musk and President Donald Trump. Expert John Murillo cites delivery misses, political risks, and high valuation as short-term pressures, noting downgrades from JPMorgan, Baird, and Argus. Despite a 21.91% year-to-date decline, Murillo sees long-term potential in robotaxis and energy storage, suggesting Tesla diversify its product line and stabilize sales in Europe and China.

IBM has outperformed the Magnificent 7 stocks in 2025 with a 32.75% year-to-date return, compared to Tesla's -16.86%. IBM's generative AI business has surpassed $5 billion. The company is evaluating strategies to mitigate potential tariff impacts, noting that direct exposure is minimal. IBM is set to report second-quarter earnings on July 24.

BNP Paribas analysts suggest Rivian and Lucid could benefit from President Trump's 'Big Beautiful Bill' as legacy automakers may slow EV production, potentially increasing market share for new-age EV companies. The bill may end the $7,500 federal EV tax credit on September 30. Tesla reported 384,211 deliveries in Q2, while Rivian produced 5,979 units and delivered 10,661, and Lucid produced 3,863 units with 3,309 deliveries.

Investors Gary Black and Ross Gerber criticized Tesla's Cybertruck sales, with Black suggesting Tesla launch a traditional pickup in the $50,000 range to capture a share of the 8-million unit global market. Tesla's Q2 deliveries of 384,000 were primarily driven by Model 3 and Model Y sales. Elon Musk has assumed oversight of sales in the U.S. and Europe following the departure of Omead Afshar.

President Trump's 'Big, Beautiful Bill,' effective July 4, eliminates ZEV credits, a revenue stream that generated $2.76 billion for Tesla in 2024. The bill also removes the $7,500 federal EV tax credit and $4,000 used EV credit, and revokes EPA waivers for state-level ZEV mandates. Tesla aims to stabilize revenue by introducing affordable models and leveraging recent Q2 delivery growth in China and the UK.

Tesla's UK sales rose 12% year-on-year in June to 7,891 units, contributing to Q2 delivery figures that exceeded analyst expectations. Total UK EV sales grew 45.5% in June. Elon Musk has taken direct oversight of Tesla sales in Europe and the U.S. following the exit of Omead Afshar.

A Quantus Insights poll conducted between June 30 and July 2 found that 40% of U.S. voters would be inclined to support a hypothetical 'America Party' launched by Elon Musk. The party was proposed by Musk in response to President Trump's 'Big, Beautiful Bill.' The poll noted significant interest among male Republicans and independent men, while older and Democratic voters remained skeptical.

Elon Musk has renewed his demand for the release of unredacted Jeffrey Epstein files, alleging that President Donald Trump is named in them. This follows a public feud between Musk and Trump regarding a tax bill Musk labeled as leading to 'debt slavery.' Trump has threatened to involve the Department of Government Efficiency against Musk and suggested he return to South Africa. The ongoing conflict could impact future legislation and regulatory decisions affecting Tesla Inc.

An old video has resurfaced showing Elon Musk discussing potential plans with Tom Cruise to film a 'Mission: Impossible' movie in space using SpaceX rockets. Amidst a public feud with President Trump, who recently removed Jared Isaacman from a NASA role, Musk's Mars ambitions face uncertainty. Additionally, Musk is managing challenges at Tesla, including declining sales and NHTSA scrutiny of its Robotaxi.

The US Air Force has suspended a project with SpaceX to test hypersonic rocket cargo deliveries from the Johnston Atoll due to environmental concerns regarding local seabird populations. The project aimed to test landing vehicles capable of delivering 100 tons of cargo globally within 90 minutes. This suspension raises questions about the broader $13.5 billion U.S. Space Force contract awarded to SpaceX, United Launch Alliance, and Blue Origin.

The House of Representatives passed President Trump's 'One Big, Beautiful Bill' with a 218-214 vote, with a signing expected on July 4. The bill eliminates the $7,500 federal EV tax credit for new vehicles and the $4,000 credit for used EVs, effective September 30. It also removes civil fines for missing CAFE norms, repeals a $250 annual EV owner fee, and cancels a USPS fleet overhaul. The bill reflects the administration's anti-EV stance, further straining the relationship between Trump and Tesla CEO Elon Musk.

Michael Cohen, former attorney for Donald Trump, reached out to Tesla CEO Elon Musk offering advice on 'political guerrilla warfare' following Musk's public feud with the President. Musk had criticized Trump's 'One Big Beautiful Bill' and alleged Trump's involvement in the Epstein files. Cohen warned Musk that Trump prioritizes his own interests above all else and that Musk made a mistake in believing he was untouchable.

Investor Kevin O'Leary stated that only one in three people have the grit to be successful entrepreneurs, while the rest can be successful employees. O'Leary praised Tesla CEO Elon Musk for his high 'signal-to-noise ratio,' describing him as having 'no noise' and focusing entirely on output, which O'Leary attributes to his success.

President Trump signed a tax and spending package into law, leading to record highs for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100. Other companies like Lucid, Rivian, and NIO also reported production and delivery figures, while Xiaomi explored overseas sales.

Tesla has outperformed the market over the last 15 years with an average annual return of 44.23%. An investment of $1,000 in Tesla 15 years ago would be worth $258,598.24 today, based on a current stock price of $294.40. The company currently holds a market capitalization of $948.25 billion.

Elon Musk has launched the American Party, a new political faction. While there is public interest in whether Musk can run for U.S. president, he is ineligible because he is not a natural-born citizen. He became a U.S. citizen in 2002 and is over 30, meaning he could legally run for the House of Representatives, the Senate, or state governor roles. Musk has expressed opposition to a spending bill signed by Donald Trump on July 4. Analysts suggest that if Musk were to pursue a political career, he would likely need to step down as CEO of Tesla, which could impact the company's stock.

The 'Big Beautiful Bill' signed by President Donald Trump will eliminate the $7,500 federal EV tax credit within months. This change impacts various EV companies, including Tesla and the Jeff Bezos-backed startup Slate Auto. Slate Auto, which initially aimed for a starting price under $20,000 including the credit, now expects pricing in the 'mid-twenties' for its customizable electric pickup. Tesla CEO Elon Musk has previously welcomed the removal of the tax credit, though analysts note it may disproportionately affect Tesla compared to competitors.

This article proposes a hypothetical 'America Party Musk Exposure Index Fund' (AMPX) consisting of companies influenced by Elon Musk's political and business activities. The piece explores the intersection of Musk's political involvement, his new party, and the potential market impact on these companies, noting that Musk's influence on policy and media could serve as a market signal.

Tesla stock has shown technical patterns of resistance and support. The stock hit resistance at $359 in February, May, and late June due to buyer's remorse. The movement follows Elon Musk's announcement of plans to launch a new political party.

SpaceX is hiring an 'AI Software Engineer, Vehicle Engineering' to develop AI systems for software, avionics, and mission operations. SpaceX plans to invest over $2 billion in Elon Musk's xAI. Global EV and plug-in hybrid sales rose 24% year-on-year in June, driven by growth in China and Europe. Conversely, U.S. EV sales fell 1% as the administration axed the $7,500 EV tax credit and relaxed CAFE standards. The slowdown in U.S. EV adoption has led Panasonic to delay plans for increased battery production in the country.

The U.S. Supreme Court cleared the way for the Trump administration to proceed with downsizing the Department of Education, potentially affecting 1,400 jobs. This initiative is part of a government efficiency effort overseen by Tesla CEO Elon Musk. The administration plans to transfer student loan portfolio management to the Small Business Administration and special education services to the Department of Health and Human Services.

Tesla has officially entered the Indian market, opening its first showroom in Mumbai. The Model Y rear-wheel drive variant is priced at approximately $70,000 (over 6 million INR), while the long-range rear-wheel drive model is priced at roughly $79,000 (6.8 million INR). Full Self-Driving (FSD) is available for an additional $7,000 (600,000 INR). The entry follows the Indian government's reduction of import duties on luxury EVs from 110% to 15%.

Elon Musk's xAI has introduced 'Grok for Government,' a suite of products for U.S. government customers. The company secured a defense contract from the Pentagon and made its products available via the General Services Administration (GSA) schedule. This follows reports of xAI receiving over $200 million in defense contracts alongside companies like Google and Anthropic.

Senator Elizabeth Warren criticized the Pentagon's $200 million contract with xAI, citing recent instances where the Grok chatbot generated antisemitic and pro-Hitler content. xAI apologized for the behavior, attributing it to a code update. Meanwhile, Elon Musk confirmed that Grok is expected to be integrated into Tesla vehicles soon.

Tesla shares rose 1.08% to close at $316.90 on Monday, amid broader market reactions to President Trump's announcement of a 30% tariff on imports from the EU and Mexico, effective August 1. Analysts noted that these trade policies could impact Tesla's global supply chains and revenue.

Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed the 'Making American Election Great Again Act,' which mandates a new census counting only U.S. citizens, requires proof of citizenship for federal voting, and directs states to redraw congressional districts based on the citizen-only count. Tesla CEO Elon Musk has previously expressed support for stricter voter ID requirements.

Tesla is reportedly preparing to launch a six-seat, extended version of the Model Y in China, roughly 150 centimeters longer than the current model. While Tesla reported 5,010 insured registrations in China during the first week of July, Gary Black noted 12.3K registrations for the week of July 7-13. BYD recently surpassed Tesla in global battery electric vehicle sales and 2024 annual revenue.

Analysis of 333 unusual Tesla options trades shows 52% bullish sentiment, with 270 calls valued at $19.6 million and 63 puts valued at $4.3 million. White House AI czar David Sacks expressed hope for reconciliation between Donald Trump and Elon Musk. The feud follows Musk's opposition to the 'One Big Beautiful Bill' and his proposal for an 'America Party.' Trump's nominee for the NHTSA, Jonathan Morrison, is expected to pursue stricter regulation of autonomous vehicles, potentially impacting Tesla's FSD and Robotaxi initiatives.

U.S. EV transaction prices fell to $56,910 in June, with record incentives of 14.8%. Tesla's U.S. sales dropped 12.6% YoY in Q2, totaling 143,535 vehicles, though it maintains a 44.7% market share. North American sales head Troy Jones and aide Omead Afshar have departed the company.

Elon Musk's 'Muskonomy' strategy, which leverages resources across Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, faces criticism from analysts like Gadjo Sevilla, who warns it could cannibalize Tesla and delay innovation. Investors are concerned about the redirection of $500 million in Nvidia chips from Tesla to X and xAI, and potential risks regarding the integration of the Grok chatbot into Tesla vehicles.

Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison has tied Mark Zuckerberg with a $251 billion net worth, trailing only Elon Musk, who remains the world's richest person at $358 billion. Oracle's stock rose 47.27% over the last six months, bolstered by AI partnerships with OpenAI and SoftBank and the U.S. government's easing of export restrictions on semiconductors to China.

Future Fund LLC's Gary Black stated a shareholder vote is the 'fairest' way to decide on a potential merger between Tesla and xAI. Black noted that while retail investors might support it, institutions would likely oppose it due to dilution concerns. Tesla plans to offer the Grok AI model in cars equipped with AMD Ryzen chipsets.

NHTSA nominee Jonathan Morrison is expected to testify to the U.S. Senate in favor of stricter oversight of autonomous vehicle technology. This shift poses potential challenges for Tesla's FSD and Robotaxi programs, which are already under scrutiny for traffic violations. Additionally, proposed budget cuts may eliminate ZEV credit revenue for EV makers.

House Speaker Mike Johnson is calling for transparency in the Jeffrey Epstein case, diverging from Donald Trump's stance of letting the matter rest. Johnson suggested the possibility of Ghislaine Maxwell testifying before Congress and subpoenaing Department of Justice documents. President Trump's approval rating stands at 44%, with 54% disapproving. 74% of Benzinga readers polled believe Musk should stay focused on his companies rather than politics. Daniel Ives named Tesla, Nvidia, Palantir, Microsoft, and Meta as his top five tech picks for the second half of 2025, citing the AI revolution. Ives expects a significant increase in enterprise AI capital expenditure.

Concerns center on the lack of published system cards and safety testing documentation for Grok 4. xAI is pursuing enterprise opportunities, including potential Pentagon contracts and integration into Tesla vehicles.

In a trial regarding a fatal 2019 accident in Key Largo, Florida, a Tesla engineer testified that the company did not maintain crash records for its Autopilot system until 2018, three years after the feature's launch. Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, attributes his success to first principles thinking, intellectual capital, and cross-industry innovation. This leadership change occurs as Tesla faces a 13% sales decline last quarter, its weakest performance in three years, attributed to competition, an aging vehicle lineup, and backlash against CEO Elon Musk's politics. Polls from YouGov and CNN indicate that while many Americans support a third political party, there is little enthusiasm for an "America Party" led by Tesla CEO Elon Musk. Only 11% to 25% of respondents expressed interest in a party associated with Musk. Despite proposing the idea following objections to legislation in Donald Trump's agenda, Musk has not taken formal steps to establish the party.

Tesla is reportedly preparing to launch a six-seat version of its Model Y SUV in China. Wall Street expects Tesla to report Q2 earnings of 42 cents per share on $22.79 billion in revenue. Tesla shares rose 0.4% to $333.44 in after-hours trading ahead of the announcement.

The Trump administration announced a 15% tariff on imported vehicles. While Toyota's stock surged 8%, American automakers including Ford, GM, and Tesla face higher costs due to additional tariffs on steel, copper, and components from Canada, Mexico, and China.

A report by Anthropic states the U.S. is trailing China in AI development due to energy constraints, noting China added 400 GW of power capacity in 2024 compared to 'several dozen' in the U.S. The U.S. AI sector requires at least 50GW of additional capacity by 2028 to maintain leadership.
Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) shares rose 0.35% in premarket trading ahead of its earnings report. Analysts expect the company to report earnings of 42 cents per share on $22.79 billion in revenue.

Tesla recorded a 21.1% year-over-year decline in California new car registrations for Q2, falling to 41,138 units. Year-to-date sales through June dropped 18.3% to 83,375, and its California market share slid to 8.5%. Despite this, Model Y and Model 3 remain top-selling EVs. Ahead of the Q2 earnings call, where analysts expect $22.79 billion in revenue, Bank of America raised its Tesla price target to $341, while Piper Sandler reiterated a $500 price target.
Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is scheduled to report its second-quarter earnings after the market closes on July 23, 2025, with projected earnings of $0.35 per share on $22.25 billion in revenue.

Gary Black of The Future Fund predicts a positive stock reaction for Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) following its Q2 earnings call on July 23, 2025. Key investor focus includes management's timeline for removing safety drivers from robotaxis in Austin to demonstrate Level 4 autonomy. Black also noted that a 6-seat Model Y variant would not expand the total addressable market. Tesla faces challenges including three consecutive quarters of missed revenue estimates, a 20% sales decline in China, a 12% U.S. sales drop in Q2, and a California DMV lawsuit regarding Autopilot and FSD advertising.

General Motors CEO Mary Barra stated that Chevrolet is the second-largest EV brand in the U.S., while Cadillac leads in luxury EV sales. GM noted that the GMC Hummer EV and Silverado EV outsold the Tesla Cybertruck in the U.S. during the period. Additionally, GM expects prices for its EVs, including the Blazer EV and Cadillac Lyriq, to increase following the removal of EV incentives.

A future A24 biopic about Elon Musk, directed by Darren Aronofsky and based on Walter Isaacson's biography, is in development. Grok suggested Christian Bale, Rami Malek, Cillian Murphy, Adam Driver, and Edward Norton as potential actors to play Musk, with Bale as the top choice for his immersion and Murphy for physical resemblance. Separately, actor Ike Barinholtz is in talks to play Musk in a different film titled 'Artificial' about OpenAI.

The technology is also intended to support Tesla Inc.'s (TSLA) Full Self-Driving ambitions. Kalshi is a CFTC-regulated exchange valued at approximately $2 billion.

Sunrun Inc. (RUN) and Tesla Electric launched a home energy plan for Texas residents enrolled in Sunrun Flex. The plan offers a fixed-rate power structure with sell-back credits for excess solar generation. Sunrun Flex installs Tesla Powerwall units for backup power and grid stability. Sunrun shares were trading at $10.46, down 1.64%, on Thursday.

Actor Ike Barinholtz is in talks to portray Elon Musk in the upcoming Amazon MGM Studios film 'Artificial', which focuses on the 2023 removal of Sam Altman from OpenAI. The project highlights Musk's public comments on OpenAI's financial situation and his past legal disputes with the company.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that chip allocation for high-profile clients like Tesla's Elon Musk is managed simply by placing a purchase order. Huang noted that Nvidia discloses its roadmap a year in advance and addressed supply concerns by mentioning he wrote off $5 billion in Hopper chips. He also commented on the industry's multi-trillion dollar infrastructure buildout.

Sony is selling its cellular chipset unit, formerly Altair Semiconductor, which it acquired for $212 million in 2016 and could be valued at $300 million. The company is shifting focus to entertainment. Separately, the Sony Honda Mobility joint venture plans to launch three electric vehicles by the late 2020s to compete with Tesla. The Afeela 1 sedan, starting at $89,900, is set for U.S. sales in 2025 with deliveries by mid-2026.

Tesla shares fell over 6% pre-market after reporting Q2 revenue of $22.5 billion, a 12% year-over-year decline. CFO Vaibhav Taneja urged customers to order vehicles immediately to avoid potential delivery delays caused by inventory shortages.

Tesla stock dropped 6.09% in pre-market trading after reporting Q2 revenue of $22.5 billion, missing the $22.8 billion consensus estimate, and earnings per share of $0.40, missing the $0.42 estimate. Gary Black of Future Fund LLC criticized Tesla's planned affordable Model Y trim, arguing it provides no incremental volume and will cannibalize higher-priced models. He noted Tesla's Q2 revenue fell 12% YoY to $22.5 billion and highlighted a 21% sales decline in California.

ETFs with high Tesla exposure, including TESL, XLY, NITE, FDIS, and VCR, experienced volatility and price dips following the report. Rivian stock rose on Friday, mirroring gains from Tesla's robotaxi news. Tesla has seen a 21% year-to-date decline amid falling sales and lack of 2025 guidance. LG Energy Solutions, a battery supplier for Tesla and General Motors, warned that U.S. tariffs and the end of EV subsidies will slow North American EV battery demand. The company plans to shift focus to energy storage production. Additionally, Panasonic has delayed production ramp-ups at its Kansas plant due to Tesla's sales slump.

Elon Musk stated a $20 trillion valuation for Tesla is possible with 'extreme execution,' despite a 12% decline in Q2 revenue to $22.5 billion and dwindling sales. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintains a $500 price target, while Gary Black criticized the Q2 earnings call as a 'disaster' and questioned the strategy for upcoming affordable vehicle models.

Pony.ai launched 24/7 Robotaxi operations in Guangzhou and Shenzhen, expanding from 15 hours daily. It also extended 24-hour testing in Beijing. The company's Gen-7 fleet uses a sensor fusion of 128-beam LiDAR, 8-megapixel cameras, and 4D imaging radar. Pony.ai plans to mass-produce 1,000 vehicles in 2025 and has surpassed 2 million km in Level 4 controller testing. Despite a 106% stock surge over three months, it faces allegations from Grizzly Research regarding data falsification.

Tesla is offering various incentives amid declining sales, including free FSD transfers, a month-long free Supervised FSD trial, and $1,000 discounts for military, teachers, first responders, and students. Additional offers include a $1,000 credit for Lyft drivers and discounts on inventory: up to $2,000 off Model Y and Model 3, and up to $8,300 off 2025 Model X vehicles. These can be stacked with a $7,500 EV credit available until the end of September. This follows a 12% revenue decline and a 23% drop in European deliveries.

Apple released the public beta of iOS 26, featuring a 'Liquid Glass' user interface redesign. The update includes AI-powered real-time translations for iMessage, FaceTime, and phone calls, and visual search capabilities. While Siri updates are excluded, the beta addresses readability concerns raised after the initial unveiling at WWDC 2025. Tesla CEO Elon Musk previously praised the design.

U.S. stock futures were mixed on Friday. Tesla reported weaker-than-expected second-quarter financial results after the market closed Wednesday. The Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates unchanged at its July 30 meeting, with a 97.4% probability according to the CME FedWatch tool. Economic data showed U.S. initial jobless claims at 217,000 and S&P Global manufacturing PMI at 49.5 for July.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman defended Palantir co-founder Peter Thiel against media criticism. Altman also expressed concerns regarding potential government surveillance and AI misuse.

Tesla reported $22.5 billion in revenue and 384,212 deliveries in Q2, with a 12% YoY revenue decline.

Elon Musk denied receiving government subsidies, stating they do not exist, and criticized Donald Trump for removing sustainable energy support while leaving oil and gas subsidies intact. Musk defended SpaceX, claiming it won NASA contracts by performing better for less money. This follows Tesla's disappointing Q2 earnings and concerns regarding the potential cannibalization of its vehicle lineup by a planned scaled-down Model Y.

Economist Justin Wolfers argued that President Trump's new trade deal with Japan effectively raises taxes on American consumers by increasing tariffs from 2% to 15%. While Japanese auto stocks like Toyota and Honda rallied, U.S. automakers face challenges; Tesla warned of 'a few rough quarters' due to tariffs, reporting $300 million in additional Q2 costs, while General Motors reported a $1.1 billion tariff-related headwind.

Elon Musk predicted that AI will increase birth rates to 'maximize the future light cone of neurotransmitter tonnage,' challenging the notion that technology leads to population decline. He noted that the U.S. birth rate has been below replacement levels since approximately 1971.

Tesla confirmed it received multiple shareholder proposals regarding a potential investment in Elon Musk's AI startup, xAI. Per SEC rules, the company will include one properly submitted proposal on the topic in its annual proxy statement for the November 6 shareholder meeting. Musk has previously stated he supports a shareholder vote on the investment. Tesla shares rose 3.52% on Friday, though they are down 21.74% year-to-date.

Gary Black, managing director of The Future Fund LLC, expressed skepticism regarding Tesla's second supervised robotaxi test in San Francisco, arguing that FSD cannot be considered fully autonomous or scaled nationally until the safety driver is removed. Tesla plans to launch the service in the Bay Area, including Marin, East Bay, San Francisco, and San Jose, with safety drivers. Despite a 22% year-to-date decline in TSLA stock, shares rose nearly 4% following the news. Black contrasted Tesla's situation with Uber, which is trading near all-time highs.

The tech industry saw several AI developments this week: Meta appointed Shengjia Zhao as Chief Scientist for its Superintelligence Labs; China proposed a global AI organization at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference; OpenAI Chairman Bret Taylor warned startups about the high costs of training new AI models; Tesla acknowledged receiving shareholder proposals regarding a potential investment in Elon Musk's AI startup, xAI; and Walmart announced a strategy to consolidate its AI agents into four unified 'super agents'.

Wall Street reached record highs amid strong earnings and trade deals, including a U.S.-Japan agreement cutting auto tariffs from 25% to 15%. Tesla reported Q2 revenue of $22.5 billion, a 12% year-over-year decline that missed estimates. Other earnings included Alphabet ($96.43B revenue), IBM ($16.977B revenue), and GM ($47.12B sales). Pony AI launched 24/7 robotaxi operations in Guangzhou and Shenzhen. Tesla is offering incentives across its vehicle lineup, and the company confirmed it received shareholder proposals regarding a potential investment in xAI.

Elon Musk stated that Tesla could reach a market capitalization of $20 trillion to $30 trillion, driven by growth in autonomous vehicles and the Optimus humanoid robot. For context, the 10 most valuable companies currently have a combined valuation of $23.2 trillion. A Benzinga poll indicated that investors prefer other Musk-led ventures over Tesla, with only 16% of respondents choosing Tesla as their top investment choice compared to 27% for Starlink and 27% for SpaceX.

Tesla shares rose 3.88% to $328.35 on Monday following reports of a $16.5 billion, eight-year contract with Samsung Electronics to produce Tesla's next-generation AI6 chip. The chips, intended for autonomous driving and humanoid robot technology, will be manufactured at Samsung's Texas plant. Elon Musk confirmed the deal on X, noting that the $16.5 billion figure is a minimum and that the chips could also be used in Tesla's data centers to train video-based AI models, reducing reliance on Nvidia and AMD.

Tesla shares jumped 4% following the announcement of a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung Electronics to produce next-generation chips and news of lower-than-expected U.S. tariffs on European automobiles.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk confirmed a $16.5 billion chip manufacturing deal with Samsung Electronics for the next-generation AI6 chip. Samsung currently manufactures the AI4 chip. Taiwan Semiconductor will produce the AI5 chip, initially in Taiwan before moving to Arizona.

Tesla's Optimus robot program faces scrutiny due to production delays, with only hundreds of units built versus a promised 5,000 by year-end, and reports of units lacking functional hands. The program is highly secretive, and analysts warn that market valuations, including Cathie Wood's $2,600 price target for 2029, may be over-relying on future AI-driven growth.

Mill City Ventures III, Ltd. announced a $450 million private placement to adopt a SUI treasury strategy, acquiring SUI tokens. The article notes that Tesla is among the companies that hold Bitcoin reserves, as reported in public SEC filings.

On July 24, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Andres Sheppard reiterated an Overweight rating on Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) with a price target of $355, representing approximately 10% upside. The rating follows Tesla's confirmation of a $16.5 billion chip manufacturing deal with Samsung Electronics.

Tesla fans traveled thousands of miles to attend an invite-only Robotaxi event in Austin last month. Tesla is reportedly planning a limited release of the Robotaxi service in the San Francisco Bay Area with an onboard safety driver. During an X takeover event, Tesla SVP of Vehicle Engineering Lars Moravy hinted at a potential new EV pickup truck.

Tesla's new retro-futuristic diner in Los Angeles has become a focal point for 'Tesla Takedown' protests. Elon Musk criticized LiDAR technology on X following a video of a Waymo robotaxi collision, contrasting it with Tesla's camera-based Full Self-Driving (FSD) system.
