Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock rose 1.72% in premarket trading on Monday following the confirmation of a $16.5 billion chip manufacturing deal with Samsung Electronics.

Tesla investors are expressing skepticism toward Elon Musk's robotaxi and autonomous driving promises following a disappointing Q2 earnings report. The stock fell 5.49% over five days amid competition from Chinese EV makers and brand image concerns. Analysts at Canaccord Genuity emphasized a need for current growth, while Goldman Sachs noted the robotaxi effort remains small with limited data. Engineering chief Lars Moravy hinted at a strategic shift toward more immediate projects like a smaller pickup truck.

BYD is facing expansion hurdles in India due to geopolitical tensions with China, including visa denials for its managing director and the rejection of a $1 billion investment plan. Conversely, Tesla has entered the Indian market with a showroom in Mumbai to sell the Model Y, while Elon Musk's Starlink has secured a license to operate in the country. Despite domestic sales slipping 8% in June, BYD has achieved over 60% of its 800,000-unit overseas sales target.

Tesla Senior VP of Vehicle Engineering Lars Moravy described the company as being in a 'big swing moment' regarding its focus on autonomy, robotaxis, Optimus, and the Semi. Moravy confirmed that Tesla is exploring designs for a smaller pickup truck. This follows a Q2 earnings report showing a 12% year-over-year revenue decline and ambitious goals to serve half the U.S. population with robotaxis by the end of 2025.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick announced that the U.S. will release results of a semiconductor import probe in two weeks. Conducted under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, the investigation addresses national security risks related to reliance on foreign-made chips. The ruling could lead to new tariffs on global chipmakers like TSMC and Samsung unless they increase U.S.-based production, impacting tech giants including Tesla, Nvidia, and Apple.

Tesla has confirmed a $16.5 billion contract with Samsung Electronics to manufacture its next-generation AI6 chips at Samsung's Texas facility. The deal runs from July 26, 2024, through Dec. 31, 2033. Elon Musk stated that TSMC will produce the AI5 chips. Musk noted that Tesla will assist in maximizing manufacturing efficiency at the Samsung plant to accelerate progress.

Elon Musk identified orbital propellant refilling as the next major engineering challenge for the Starship rocket, describing it as one of the hardest tasks alongside developing a fully reusable orbital heat shield. Musk emphasized that achieving full reusability for the rocket and booster would significantly reduce costs. SpaceX is preparing for the next Starship launch scheduled for August.

Tesla shares traded lower as investors balanced AI ambitions against Q2 financial results, which showed a 12% year-over-year revenue decline to $22.5 billion. The company confirmed a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung for AI6 chip production to support future autonomous products. RBC Capital maintained an Outperform rating with a $325 price target, while Benzinga Edge rankings show strong quality and momentum scores but a low value score of 10.15.

Li Auto launched the Li i8, a six-seat battery electric family SUV, with deliveries starting August 20, 2025. Carvana reported that EVs and PHEVs accounted for 9% of its unit sales in Q2 2025, up from 2% in 2023. Tesla models (Model 3, Model Y, and Model S) held the top three spots in used EV sales on the platform. Meanwhile, Tesla is experiencing sales declines, including a 21% drop in California and a 60% decline in the UK in July. Used car prices have risen 6.3% year-over-year, partly attributed to tariffs.

Tesla has shut down its Dojo Supercomputer team, with leader Peter Bannon departing. Over 20 employees left for DensityAI. Tesla plans to outsource AI needs to companies like Nvidia, AMD, and Samsung, having signed a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung for AI6 chips. Additionally, Tesla awarded CEO Elon Musk 96 million shares valued at $29 billion. The company faces sales declines in the UK, France, Sweden, and Denmark, and lacks permits for robotaxi operations in the San Francisco Bay Area.

The U.S. Air Force is purchasing two Tesla Cybertrucks for missile target practice at the White Sands Missile Range. Tesla sold 4,306 Cybertrucks in Q2 2025, a 51% quarterly decline, with an annual run rate of 20,000 units against a 250,000 target. The vehicle has faced eight recalls in 14 months and currently has nearly $200 million in unsold inventory. The starting price is $60,990, higher than the originally promised $39,900.

Elon Musk's net worth dropped $61.2 billion in 2025 to $371 billion, largely due to Tesla's stock falling 13.08% year-to-date amid declining sales. Tesla's Q2 revenue was $22.5 billion, a 12% year-over-year decline, with automotive revenue falling 16% to $16.66 billion. Bill Gates also saw a $35.7 billion decline, though this is attributed to his commitment to donate his fortune to the Gates Foundation.

Tesla Robotaxi LLC received a permit to operate as a transportation network company in Texas. The service, using Model Y vehicles with human safety supervisors, has been operational in Austin since late June. The permit allows operation without a human safety driver or valet. Tesla is also expanding its Robotaxi service to Phoenix, Arizona, with CEO Elon Musk aiming to serve half the U.S. population by the end of 2025. TSLA stock closed at $329.65 and rose 0.14% in after-hours trading.

President Donald Trump has defended Tesla CEO Elon Musk amid backlash regarding his political activities. Tesla has discontinued its Dojo Supercomputer team, which was responsible for developing in-house AI capabilities, including self-driving chips and AI model training.

Tesla continues to lead used EV sales on Carvana, with EVs and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) accounting for a record 9% of unit sales in the second quarter of 2025, up from just over 2% in the same period in 2023. Additionally, Tesla is offering free lifetime Supercharging with the Foundation Series Cybertruck to reduce inventory.

A July Gallup poll of 1,000 American adults found that 61% of respondents hold a negative view of Elon Musk, leaving him last in favorability among 14 world figures. His favorable rating dropped to 33%, a 24-point decline since January. The poll suggests his controversial actions and involvement in the Department of Government Efficiency have negatively impacted his image, which may influence consumer behavior and investor confidence in Tesla.

On August 11, Tesla (TSLA) stock triggered a 'Power Inflow' trading signal at $341.10, an indicator of institutional order flow. Following the signal, the stock rose as much as 1.6% to a high of $346.48, though it closed at $339.03. The signal is interpreted by active traders as a bullish indicator of potential upward momentum.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk identified Rivian as the largest cash burner among non-Tesla EV manufacturers, noting that non-Tesla EV makers have collectively lost $88 billion in free cash flow. While Tesla remains cash flow positive, the article notes that Tesla's sales have declined by double digits in several global markets, including over 60% in the U.K. and over 21% in California.

Tesla CFO Vaibhav Taneja noted in a late June earnings call that tariffs increased costs by approximately $300 million, with about two-thirds impacting the automotive segment. Experts suggest investors should balance portfolios by including companies strong in electric vehicles, specifically naming Tesla and BYD, alongside traditional automakers like GM that are adapting to market needs.

Bank of America's August Fund Manager Survey identifies the 'Magnificent Seven'—including Tesla (TSLA)—as the most crowded trade, cited by 45% of respondents. Despite 91% of participants viewing U.S. stocks as overvalued, sentiment remains bullish, with equity allocations reaching a net 14% overweight.

Tesla experienced a decline in Chinese domestic sales in July, with Model Y sales falling 15.24% year-over-year to 30,766 units (down 31.4% from June) and Model 3 sales dropping 40.79% compared to June. Global sales also saw double-digit declines in Sweden, Denmark, and France, a 60% drop in the UK, and a 21% decrease in California.

Elon Musk confirmed on X that Tesla will reach volume production of the Semi truck in 2026. Additionally, reports indicate Tesla may be planning a European rollout for the Semi and is exploring designs for a smaller EV pickup truck. Tesla has also scrapped in-house Dojo AI chip development in favor of a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung Electronics for AI6 chips.

Tesla is one of the 'Magnificent Seven' companies credited with driving over half of the S&P 500's total growth in 2023 and 2024. Tesla's year-to-date performance is -13.08%, with a one-year return of 66.93% and a five-year return of 199.57%.

CATL, a battery supplier for Tesla and General Motors, has temporarily halted operations at its Jianxiawo lithium mine in China for three months due to an expired permit. The mine accounts for 6% of global lithium output, and analysts suggest the closure may be part of a Chinese government initiative to regulate resources and prevent overcapacity.

Tesla has applied to the UK regulator Ofgem for a license to supply electricity to homes and businesses under the name Tesla Electric. The service would integrate with Tesla vehicles and Powerwall batteries. This move comes as Tesla's UK EV sales dropped nearly 60% in July 2025 compared to the previous year, with similar declines across Sweden, Denmark, and France. The UK government recently committed £63 million to EV infrastructure, though subsidies may exclude luxury brands like Tesla.

Elon Musk expressed optimism for Tesla's AI future despite the company ending its in-house Dojo chip program. Tesla has signed a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung Electronics to develop AI6 chips for its self-driving technology. Over 20 employees recently left the Dojo program to join DensityAI. Additionally, Tesla secured a permit to operate its ride-hailing service in Texas, though it faces a lawsuit from investors regarding the safety of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system.

Elon Musk stated that maintaining positive free cash flow at Tesla is 'harder than it looks,' noting that competitors like Rivian and Lucid have accrued $88 billion in cumulative cash burn. Despite Tesla's positive cash flow, the company is experiencing double-digit sales declines in France, the UK, and California. Tesla recently secured a permit to operate ride-hailing services in Texas, supporting Musk's goal to serve over half the U.S. population with Robotaxis by year-end.

A security researcher, Eaton Zveare, discovered a vulnerability in an unnamed major automaker's online dealership portal that allowed for the creation of administrative accounts. This flaw enabled unauthorized access to confidential financial data, vehicle tracking, and the ability to remotely unlock car doors. The report highlights ongoing cybersecurity challenges in the auto industry, which has seen attacks on over 15,000 U.S. dealerships and is currently navigating market uncertainties including tariffs and declining sales for companies like Tesla.

Congressman Cleo Fields disclosed significant investments in six of the 'Magnificent Seven' stocks in July 2025, totaling millions of dollars. Nvidia was his largest holding, with purchases between $1.48 million and $3.45 million. Tesla was the only Magnificent Seven stock not included in his recent purchases. A recent Benzinga poll indicated that readers view Nvidia as the top long-term investment among the group, while Tesla ranked last.

Mainland China EV sales reached 1.26 million units in July, a 5% month-on-month decline but a 27.4% year-on-year increase. Beijing is pushing for reduced discounts to improve profitability. From August 4 to 10, Tesla registered 13,400 vehicles in China, a 21.6% increase from the previous week. Other manufacturers like Nio, Xpeng, and Li Auto saw varying registration results, with most experiencing week-over-week declines.

Tesla recorded 13,400 insured registrations in China from August 4 to August 10, a 71% increase compared to the previous quarter, though still down 11% year-over-year. In July, Tesla sold 40,617 units in China, a 12% year-over-year decline, with Model Y and Model 3 sales falling 15% and over 30% respectively. European sales are also down, including a 60% drop in the UK. Tesla's average transaction value in July was $52,949, down 9.1% year-over-year.

The Trump administration released new guidelines for $5 billion in NEVI program funding for EV charging infrastructure, removing mandates for rural/disadvantaged area construction and labor/safety requirements. This follows a court injunction against a previous spending freeze. Tesla and GM, which use LG Energy Solutions batteries, faced demand declines amid the freeze and tariffs. Tesla led price reductions in July as the $7,500 Federal EV Credit is set to expire on September 30.

Piero Landolfi, Tesla's North American Director of Service, has resigned after nearly nine years to join Nimble. This follows other high-profile exits, including Omead Afshar and Troy Jones. Elon Musk is currently overseeing North American and European sales, with Raj Jegganathan appointed to lead sales. Tesla is experiencing global sales declines, including a 21% drop in California and double-digit decreases in the UK, Denmark, Sweden, and France.

U.S. new EV transaction prices fell 2.2% in July to $55,689, with incentives rising 40% as automakers prepare for the September 30 expiration of the $7,500 Federal EV Credit. Tesla led price cuts, with its average transaction price falling 9.1% year-over-year to $52,949. Despite Tesla's sales declines in China (15-40% YoY) and the UK (over 60%), it continues to dominate U.S. used EV sales.

General Motors is reviving its Cruise autonomous driving program, focusing on personal vehicles with a 'hands-free, eyes-free' system led by former Tesla executive Sterling Anderson. GM is also sourcing LFP batteries from CATL for its $35,000 Chevrolet Bolt EV and investing $888 million in V-8 engine production. Meanwhile, Tesla has secured a permit to operate ride-hailing services in Texas.

Elon Musk threatened legal action against Apple, alleging antitrust violations for suppressing his xAI Grok app in the App Store in favor of OpenAI's ChatGPT. Musk noted that while X is a top news app and Grok is ranked #5, they are excluded from the 'Must Have' section. Musk previously stated that if Apple integrates ChatGPT at the OS level, his companies, including Tesla, would stop using Apple devices due to security concerns.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk praised Ford for its historical role in inventing mass manufacturing after Ford announced a new efficient assembly process for a planned $30,000 electric pickup truck due in 2027. Ford's Doug Field, a former Tesla executive, also referenced Musk's 'the best part is no part' design philosophy. The two companies continue to compete while acknowledging mutual respect for their respective manufacturing innovations.

Xiaomi's YU7 electric SUV has seen high demand with 240,000 reservations in 18 hours, leading CEO Lei Jun to recommend competitors like the Tesla Model Y to customers due to 56-59 week wait times. The YU7 starts at $35,300 with a 519-mile range, challenging the Tesla Model Y RWD. Tesla's China deliveries fell 11.7% year-over-year in Q2 to 128,803 units despite Model Y refreshes and discounts.

Perplexity AI is employing a 'PR arbitrage' strategy similar to Tesla's early years, using bold, premature announcements to gain attention. Examples include bids for TikTok and Google Chrome, and acquisition rumors involving Apple and Meta. While Tesla's early stunts, such as the 2016 Solar Roof and 2019 Cybertruck, eventually aligned with execution, Perplexity faces the challenge of converting its hype-first model into strategic results.

Tesla is hiring 'Prototype Vehicle Operator' roles in NYC to test its Robotaxi, offering $25.25–$30.60 per hour, with potential for up to $33/hour including shift differentials. The role requires 5-8 hours of daily driving for data collection. Meanwhile, Tesla's ride-hailing service has expanded to the SF Bay Area with safety drivers, and the company faces a class-action lawsuit regarding Robotaxi safety. Additionally, Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package case is scheduled for a court hearing in October.

The Tesla Diner in Hollywood, which opened in July 2025, has significantly reduced its menu to five sandwiches, two sides, and two pie flavors, while cutting fountain-drink options. Operating hours have been adjusted to 6 a.m. to midnight, and non-Tesla owners are reportedly barred from charging. Chef Eric Greenspan attributed the changes to 'unprecedented demand.' The site has also been a target for 'Tesla Takedown' protests.

Ukraine's First Vice Prime Minister Mykhailo Fedorov confirmed a successful test of SpaceX's Starlink Direct-to-Cell technology, which aims to provide satellite connectivity to smartphones. This development occurs amid political pressure, including threats from the Trump administration to cut access over mineral rights disputes and concerns from Sen. Maggie Hassan regarding criminal use of the service. Meanwhile, European competitor Eutelsat is exploring potential roles in Ukraine.

Global EV sales reached 1.6 million in July, a 21% year-on-year increase, though down 9% from May 2025. China maintains a 50% EV penetration rate. North American sales grew 2% YTD, with expectations of short-term growth before the $7,500 Federal EV Credit expires on September 30. Tesla reported sales declines in multiple markets, including a 21% drop in California, while BYD saw over 300% growth in the UK.

Investor Gene Munster suggests Elon Musk will not need to sue Apple over Grok's App Store ranking, predicting Apple may feature the app within two weeks to avoid litigation. Musk had threatened legal action, alleging antitrust violations regarding Apple's partnership with OpenAI. Separately, Tesla has reportedly scrapped its in-house Dojo AI chip development in favor of a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung for AI6 chips and plans to integrate Grok into its vehicles.

Figure AI demonstrated its Figure 02 robot autonomously folding laundry using its 'Helix' neural net, which requires no architecture changes for new tasks. This contrasts with Tesla's Optimus, which previously showcased laundry folding via teleoperation rather than autonomy. Musk has stated that Optimus will eventually achieve full autonomy, though no timeline was provided. Optimus is targeted as a mass-market robot priced under $20,000.

The Delaware Supreme Court will hear oral arguments on October 15 regarding Elon Musk's $56 billion compensation package, which was previously struck down by Judge Kathleen McCormick. Tesla has announced a new $29 billion compensation package for Musk, consisting of over 96 million shares, and plans to hold an annual shareholder meeting in November. Additionally, Tesla faces a $243 million court-ordered payment for a 2019 Autopilot crash, a potential class-action lawsuit regarding its Robotaxi, and declining global sales.

Deepwater Asset Management's Gene Munster predicts Apple will be a top two performer among the 'Mag 7' stocks in 2025, citing potential growth from the September 2025 'iPhone Air' launch and low investor expectations for its AI capabilities. While Apple's year-to-date performance is -5.82%, Munster believes the company is well-positioned to beat Wall Street's 5% revenue growth projection. Tesla's year-to-date performance is -10.13%.

Tesla is reportedly integrating Epic Games' Unreal Engine into its Autopilot visualization software for AMD-equipped Model S and Model X vehicles, as indicated by code in the 2025.20 firmware update. This upgrade aims to provide more realistic driver visualizations. The partnership aligns with the relationship between Elon Musk and Epic CEO Tim Sweeney, and follows the inclusion of the Tesla Cybertruck in Epic's 'Fortnite' and 'Rocket League' games.

As of August 13, Tesla remains the top holding across Ark Invest's six main ETFs, representing $1.4 billion or 8.9% of the combined assets. Cathie Wood's Ark Invest maintains a long-term bullish outlook on Tesla, which is the primary holding in the ARKK, ARKQ, and ARKW funds. Other top holdings include Coinbase, Roku, Shopify, and Palantir.

Elon Musk warned Tesla short sellers that they will be 'obliterated' if they do not exit their positions before the company achieves autonomy at scale. Data shows 71.99 million shares are currently short, valued at over $24 billion. While Tesla's stock is down 11.2% year-to-date in 2025, it has risen 67% over the last year. Grok suggests large-scale autonomy could arrive by late 2025 or early 2026.

Tesla shares are trading lower following a second-quarter earnings report that missed expectations, with revenue falling 12% year-over-year to $22.5 billion and EPS of 40 cents. The company faces international competition and softening demand in Europe, though it continues to hire for its Robotaxi service and plans to launch an affordable model in the first half of 2025. Benzinga Edge data shows Tesla has strong Momentum, Growth, and Quality scores but a very low Value score of 9.27.

Zeekr reported Q2 revenue of 27.43 billion Chinese yuan ($3.83 billion), a 0.9% Y/Y decrease but a 24.6% Q/Q increase. Total vehicle deliveries reached 130,866 units, up 9.3% Y/Y. Adjusted net loss per ADS was 1.42 Chinese yuan ($0.20), down 81.2% Y/Y. Vehicle margin improved to 17.3% from 11.5% in the prior year quarter, while gross margin rose to 20.6%. As of June 30, 2025, cash and equivalents totaled 10.21 billion Chinese yuan ($1.43 billion).

BYD sold over 3,000 units in India in the first week of August, matching its total 2024 sales in the country. Expansion faces hurdles due to strained India-China relations and rejected investment proposals. Meanwhile, Tesla is entering the Indian market with a Mumbai showroom, pricing the Model Y at $70,000. BYD also reported a 314% sales surge in the UK in July, while Tesla faces global sales declines in markets including France, Sweden, and Denmark.

Tesla sales in Norway rose 24% YTD in the first half of 2025, with 13,039 units registered compared to 10,498 in the same period last year. This contrasts with double-digit sales declines in Sweden, France, and Denmark, and a 60% drop in UK sales for July. Globally, EV sales in July exceeded 1.6 million units, with China reaching 50% EV penetration.

Vice President JD Vance signaled a potential reconciliation between the MAGA contingent and Tesla CEO Elon Musk. Despite tense relations, Musk donated over $15 million to the GOP as of June. The article notes that Trump's proposed policies, including axing the $7,500 Federal EV credit and relaxing CAFE norms, could impact Tesla's business. Additionally, SpaceX was omitted from recent details regarding the Pentagon's Golden Dome missile defense system.

The article outlines four ways to invest in startups: direct angel investment, equity crowdfunding platforms, venture capital funds, and secondary markets. It notes that 90% of startups fail, but successful ones can generate high returns. Tesla is cited as an example of a company that revolutionized an industry after starting as a high-risk venture.

Tesla has achieved an average annual return of 21.46% over the past five years, outperforming the market by 7.75% on an annualized basis. With a current market capitalization of $1.07 trillion and a stock price of $332.53, a $1,000 investment in Tesla five years ago would be worth $2,717.28 today.

The Trump administration's rollback of fuel economy rules has frozen approximately $100 million in revenue for Rivian due to the NHTSA halting paperwork for credit deals. Tesla previously earned $2.76 billion from ZEV credits in 2024, but this revenue stream was eliminated by the tax bill.

Samsung is investing 25 billion yen ($170 million) in a new chip packaging R&D center in Yokohama, Japan, set to open in March 2027. Samsung recently secured a $16.5 billion foundry contract for Tesla's AI6 chips, which analysts believe will support Tesla's Robotaxi, Full Self-Driving, and Optimus programs by 2027.

Nio's Firefly brand began European deliveries in Norway and the Netherlands on August 14, 2025. Nio plans to expand to six additional European markets by year-end. Tesla and BYD are also exploring compact EV formats for international markets.

Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. denied rumors of a 2028 presidential run. Meanwhile, Steve Bannon is reportedly considering a 2028 bid, potentially challenging Vice President JD Vance. The article notes that Bannon has a strained relationship with Tesla CEO Elon Musk.

Tesla Senior VP Tom Zhu has sold over 82% of his Tesla holdings since 2023, reducing his stake from over 81,000 shares to under 15,000. Analyst Gordon Johnson called the sell-off alarming. Tesla shares closed at $330.56 on Friday, down 1.50% for the day and 12.85% year-to-date. Short interest in Tesla currently totals 71.99 million shares.

Vivian Jenna Wilson, the estranged daughter of Elon Musk, stated in an interview that she is not the cause of her father's political shift toward conservatism. She addressed Musk's public comments regarding her gender-affirming care and his transition into right-wing politics.
Tesla sales in Norway surged during the first half of the year, despite a broader European slump and a decline in Elon Musk's popularity linked to his ties with the Trump administration.
Tesla is hiring test operators for its Robotaxi program in New York City, offering pay of up to $33 per hour as part of CEO Elon Musk's expansion plans for the sector.

Tesla shares closed up 1.39% at $335.16 on Monday. Tesla is the largest holding in Cathie Wood's Ark Invest, but Warren Buffett has famously avoided investing in the company. Both investors hold shares of Tesla rival BYD Co. and Amazon.com Inc.

Pony.AI is expanding its robotaxi fleet, reporting a 158% year-over-year revenue increase in Q2. ARK Invest, led by Cathie Wood, invested $12.9 million in the company. Goldman Sachs issued a buy rating with a $24.50 price target. The company aims to operate 1,000 vehicles by year-end for unit-level profitability and has integrated ride-hailing into Tencent's WeChat.

In Q2 2025, hedge funds increased holdings in major tech stocks including Apple, Amazon, Nvidia, Microsoft, Meta, Alphabet, Tesla, and Broadcom. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) was the most purchased ETF, with significant inflows from firms like Proficio Capital Partners ($7.49 billion) and Farallon Capital Management ($3.24 billion).

S&P 500 Q2 2025 earnings grew 11% year-over-year, beating analyst expectations. The 'Magnificent Seven' (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Tesla, and Nvidia) drove this growth with a 26% earnings increase, compared to 7% for the rest of the index. Goldman Sachs reports that 2026 EPS forecasts for the Magnificent Seven have been raised 1%, while estimates for the remaining 493 stocks were cut by 4%.

Strategy Inc. added 430 Bitcoin to its treasury for $51.4 million at an average price of $119,666, bringing its total holdings to 629,376 BTC. For comparison, Tesla holds approximately 11,509 BTC, Coinbase holds 11,776 BTC, and Marathon Digital Holdings holds 50,000 BTC.
Technical analysis for Tesla (TSLA) indicates a position at 328.50. Bullish targets are set at 330.78, 334.46, and 340.39. Bearish support levels are identified at 326.66, 324.82, and 323.43.

Global new energy vehicle (NEV) sales grew 30% in Q2 2025. BYD led the market with an 18.3% share, while Tesla's share fell to 11.7% following a 14% year-over-year sales decline. Tesla sales dropped by double digits in France, Sweden, and Denmark, and by 21% in California. Tesla also cut UK lease prices by over 40% as sales there fell by over 60%.

Uber users are increasingly choosing Waymo robotaxis over human-driven rides. Uber plans to expand its autonomous fleet in Atlanta. Uber also signed a deal with Lucid Group to deploy over 20,000 robotaxis over six years. Morgan Stanley predicts the U.S. autonomous driving market could reach $200 billion by 2030. Former Waymo CEO John Krafcik criticized Tesla's ride-hailing service for requiring a safety driver.

Former Tesla Supercharger team members Harry Fox, Connor Selwood, and Hugh Leckie founded Hubber, a startup targeting the urban charging gap for passenger and fleet vehicles. The company secured approximately $81 million in funding and plans to open its first London station on August 20. Meanwhile, Tesla is slashing UK lease payments to address poor sales and inventory issues.

Former Waymo CEO John Krafcik criticized Tesla's ride-hailing service, stating it is not a true 'Robotaxi' because it requires an onboard employee. Elon Musk responded on X, suggesting critics will eventually view Tesla's self-driving as 'unfairly good.' Tesla currently operates an invite-only service in the San Francisco Bay Area with safety drivers. While UK sales fell over 60% in July, Tesla saw a 24% sales increase in Norway.

Tesla is offering up to 40% discounts to UK leasing companies to reduce inventory, potentially lowering monthly payments for a Model 3 to as low as £252. Retail prices remain unchanged, and the company is offering zero-interest financing. UK sales dropped over 60% in July, with declines also seen in France, Denmark, and Sweden, though Norway saw a 24% sales increase.

The Gartner Digital Automaker Index 2025 highlights that software-first manufacturers are leading the industry, with Chinese firms averaging a 53% digital score compared to 50% for U.S. companies and 33% for Europe. ETFs like KARS, DRIV, IDRV, and CARZ provide exposure to these digital-native automakers, including Tesla, Nio, XPeng, and Li Auto, as the industry shifts toward subscription software and AI-driven features.

Weakening U.S. EV demand and the rollback of the $7,500 federal tax credit are impacting the industry. ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) is outperforming the Nasdaq 100 by 30% over the last five months, driven by AI, robotics, and energy storage. KindlyMD Inc. acquired 5,744 Bitcoin for $679 million at an average price of $118,204.88 per coin, bringing its total treasury to 5,765 BTC. This follows the company's merger with Nakamoto Holdings in August 2025. The company joins other institutional holders like MicroStrategy, which held 629,376 BTC as of August 2025, and Tesla, which has held Bitcoin since 2021.

Nio Inc. cut the price of its 100-kWh long-range battery pack by 20,000 yuan ($2,780) to 108,000 yuan, effective August 19, following Tesla's launch of a six-seat Model Y L. Nio also announced plans to enter Singapore, Uzbekistan, and Costa Rica between 2025 and 2026, and is preparing to launch its first right-hand drive vehicle.

XPeng reported Q2 2025 revenue of 18.27 billion yuan ($2.55 billion), a 125.3% year-over-year increase, with 103,181 vehicle deliveries. The company's vehicle margin reached 14.3%, up from 6.4% a year ago. XPeng projects Q3 deliveries between 113,000 and 118,000 units. The stock is up over 68% year-to-date.

Tesla recorded 14,000 new insured registrations in China from August 11 to 17, a 4.5% weekly increase and a 60% surge compared to the previous quarter. Despite this, YTD sales are down 5.9%. Tesla recently released the 6-seater Model Y L (339,000 RMB) and the long-range Model 3+ in China. Meanwhile, UK sales dropped 60% in July, while Norway saw a 24% surge in the first half of the year.

Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene is under scrutiny for failing to disclose a book agreement with Winning Team Publishing on her 2024 financial disclosure, despite receiving $178,229.99 in royalties. Ethics experts note this could lead to civil or criminal penalties. The disclosure also highlighted her stock holdings, including Tesla and Palantir, the latter of which has surged 142% since April.

Tesla insiders, including CFO Vaibhav Taneja and SVP Tom Zhu, have sold over 50% of their equity in the company over the past year. Board members Robyn Denholm and Kimbal Musk offloaded their entire options stakes. This comes amid Tesla's sales challenges, including a 60% drop in UK sales in July and the launch of the 6-seater Model Y L in China for 339,000 RMB.

Waymo is testing a new Robotaxi featuring sixth-generation hardware, including 13 cameras, 6 radars, and 4 LiDAR sensors. While Tesla CEO Elon Musk aims to serve over half the U.S. population with Robotaxis by year-end, former Waymo CEO John Krafcik criticized Tesla's efforts, noting that Tesla vehicles require a safety driver at all times.

xAI is investing $30-$40 billion in its Memphis Colossus 1 supercomputer site, which is powered by over 208 Tesla Megapacks, each with 3.9 MWh capacity. The site plans to break ground on a 500-acre solar farm in September and use a ceramic membrane bioreactor to supply 13 million gallons of water daily for cooling. Meanwhile, Tesla has scrapped its in-house Dojo program, signing a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung Electronics for AI6 autonomous driving chips.

Senator Elizabeth Warren criticized SpaceX for paying minimal federal taxes despite receiving billions in government contracts, citing a New York Times report that the company avoided taxes on $5 billion in income using net operating loss carryforwards. Tesla launched the 6-seater Model Y L in China for 339,000 RMB ($47,184), featuring an 82 kWh battery, all-wheel drive, and over 467 miles of CLTC range. The company has deployed over 1,000 vehicles since 2013 and is partnering with Lyft to launch services in the U.K. and Germany next year. While the division is not yet profitable, it has achieved per-vehicle profitability in cities like Wuhan. Meanwhile, competitors like Zoox are seeking to deploy 2,500 driverless robotaxis, and a partnership between Lucid, Uber, and Nuro plans a U.S. rollout next year. Tesla is also preparing its Cybercab pending regulatory approval.

President Trump announced new tariffs effective October 1, including 100% on pharmaceuticals, 25% on imported heavy trucks, 50% on cabinets, and 30% on upholstered furniture. Semiconductor manufacturers may face 100% tariffs if they do not maintain a 1:1 ratio of domestic to overseas chip production. Personal spending rose 0.6% and personal income rose 0.4%, both exceeding consensus. In early trade, money flows were positive for Tesla, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, and NVIDIA, but negative for Apple.

Tesla has achieved an average annual return of 46.35% over the last 15 years, outperforming the market by 33.92% on an annualized basis. With a current market capitalization of $1.41 trillion and a share price of $423.46, a $1,000 investment in Tesla 15 years ago would be worth $308,344.66 today.

Wall Street analysts updated ratings and price targets for several companies. Deutsche Bank analyst Edison Yu maintained a Buy rating on Tesla and raised the price target from $345 to $435; Tesla shares closed at $423.39 on Thursday. Other notable changes included upgrades for ONE Gas and Riot Platforms, and downgrades for Duke Energy and CarMax.

Starting in 2026, the Trump administration plans to implement a $100,000 fee for new H-1B visa applications. While billionaire donors Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy have previously advocated for admitting more foreign tech workers, Musk has remained silent on this specific policy. Tesla is currently facing a class action lawsuit alleging it favors foreign visa holders over U.S. citizens in hiring and firing decisions.

Li Auto launched the Li i6 SUV at 249,800 yuan (~$35,020) with deliveries starting September 27, 2025. Li Auto's Q2 2025 revenue fell 4.5% to $4.2 billion, missing expectations, with adjusted EPS of $0.19. Q3 projections include $3.5-$3.7 billion in revenue and 90,000-95,000 vehicle deliveries. Deutsche Bank analysts forecast Tesla will deliver ~72,000 vehicles in China this September, while Li Auto is expected to deliver ~35,000.

Xpeng shares rose after announcing expansion into five European markets with production starting Q3 2025 via Magna International. Xpeng delivered 24,702 units internationally from January to August 2025, a 137% YoY increase. Total 2025 YTD sales reached 271,615 vehicles. The company also launched the P7 sedan in China for $30,000 to compete with the Tesla Model 3, receiving over 10,000 orders in seven minutes.

Tesla urged the EPA not to repeal the 2009 Endangerment Finding, warning that removing emissions standards would disrupt the industry and threaten U.S. market leadership. Tesla previously earned $2.8 billion in revenue from selling ZEV credits to other automakers, a stream now threatened by the Trump administration's relaxation of CAFE standards. Rivian also reported a $100 million revenue loss due to these relaxed standards.

Ross Gerber challenged Elon Musk to refund customers $10,000 for Full Self-Driving (FSD) if the technology fails to achieve Level 4/5 autonomy. Tesla faces multiple lawsuits regarding FSD safety and effectiveness. While Musk aims to serve over half the U.S. population with Robotaxis by the end of 2025, investor Gary Black suggests a new approach to valuing the service is needed. Tesla currently operates ride-hailing in California and has testing permission in Arizona.

Senator Bernie Sanders criticized the tech industry's AI and robotics push, warning of massive job losses. He specifically mentioned Tesla CEO Elon Musk, Meta's Mark Zuckerberg, OpenAI's Sam Altman, and Oracle's Larry Ellison. Tesla has prioritized AI and robotics in its Master Plan IV, with Musk stating the Optimus robot line could represent 80% of the company's future value.

Bill Gates pledged $912 million to the Global Fund to fight AIDS, tuberculosis, and malaria for 2026–2028. He noted that global development assistance for health fell 21% between 2024 and 2025 to a 15-year low. Gates criticized Tesla CEO Elon Musk's proposal for $2 trillion in government budget cuts via the Department of Government Efficiency, warning that such reductions in foreign aid could lead to millions of preventable deaths.

President Donald Trump stated he anticipates more indictments following the Justice Department's indictment of former FBI Director James Comey. Tesla CEO Elon Musk publicly backed Trump on social media following the Comey indictment.

Xiaomi CEO Lei Jun revealed that the company purchased three Tesla Model Y vehicles earlier this year for detailed disassembly and component analysis. Lei noted the Model Y is an impressive car but highlighted that Xiaomi's YU7 SUV features 800V architecture, allowing an 80% charge in 13 minutes compared to the Model Y's 18 minutes. Tesla's China sales fell 4% year-over-year to 83,200 units in August, while Xiaomi's YU7 received over 240,000 preorders within 24 hours of its June launch.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk publicly supported the Trump administration following the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey. Musk shared an AI-generated image on X referencing a previous post by Comey and expressed support for the Trump administration's stance on a potential government shutdown, agreeing with Vice President JD Vance's criticism of Democrats. This follows a recent meeting between Musk and Trump at Charlie Kirk's memorial service.

Tesla secured a production patent for its upcoming Cybercab, which will utilize an 'unboxed' assembly process. Deutsche Bank analysts forecast a 27% increase in Tesla's September deliveries in China compared to August. Additionally, a Canadian teenager reportedly failed a driving test while using a Tesla Model Y, and there are signs of a potential market resurgence for Tesla in Europe.
Tesla has secured a patent for the production of its upcoming Cybercab. Recent data indicates that Tesla's sales in the Chinese market continued to grow during the third week of September.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk and President Donald Trump met at Charlie Kirk's memorial service, sparking reconciliation speculation. Tesla Inc (TSLA) is a shared holding in both the Dan Ives Wedbush AI Revolution ETF (IVES) and the Granny Shots US Large Cap ETF (GRNY). Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) is urging customers to lock in purchases by September 30 to qualify for the $7,500 Federal EV credit. The IRS clarified that the credit remains available if a nominal payment, such as a down payment or trade-in, is made by the deadline, even if delivery occurs later. For cash purchases, Tesla allows delivery after the deadline, but leasing customers must take delivery by September 30. Additionally, California has abandoned plans for bespoke state EV incentives in favor of infrastructure improvements.

U.S. used EV sales rose 59% year-over-year in August, reaching 40,960 units, as the September 30 federal tax credit deadline approaches. The average transaction price for a used EV is $34,700. Tesla is the top-selling used EV brand on Carvana. Tesla also introduced $0 down leases for used Model 3 and Model Y vehicles in California and Texas, subject to a $695 acquisition fee.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk indicated that the 'Actually Smart Summon' feature will be updated to function in multi-level parking garages. The company faces criticism from investors like Ross Gerber, who has called for $10,000 refunds for FSD, and is currently involved in lawsuits regarding FSD, including a case in Beijing. Despite these challenges, Musk aims to serve over half the U.S. population with Robotaxis by the end of 2025.

YouTuber Marques Brownlee (MKBHD) cancelled one of his two reservations for the second-generation Tesla Roadster after waiting eight years. He initially paid $50,000 for the reservation, which he has since received back. Brownlee expressed pessimism regarding the vehicle's delivery, noting the lack of updates since its 2017 announcement and the difficulty of the cancellation process, which required phone calls rather than an online button.

Ford CEO Jim Farley identified Chinese EV manufacturers, including BYD, Geely, Nio, and Xiaomi, as a major competitive threat to Tesla and other American automakers due to their scale, cost advantages, and tech integration. Tesla faces a challenging environment in China, its second-largest market, as local rivals aggressively export and innovate. Ford CEO Jim Farley stated that Chinese automakers like BYD and Xiaomi, rather than Tesla, pose the greatest threat to the U.S. EV industry due to their innovation and low costs. Ford shares rose 0.67% to $12.09 on Monday, with a 25.28% year-to-date increase.

QuantumScape (QS) partnered with Corning (GLW) to develop ceramic separator manufacturing for solid-state batteries. QuantumScape stock rose 5.65% to $13.08 premarket following the news. While no deal is confirmed, speculation persists regarding a potential partnership between QuantumScape and Tesla, fueled by comments from QuantumScape's CEO regarding Tesla's patents.

Tesla recorded 19,300 new insured registrations in China during the week of September 22–28, its highest weekly total for the quarter. Additionally, Tesla owners in Australia and New Zealand have driven over 621,000 miles using Supervised FSD since its launch less than two weeks ago.

Tesla unveiled its V4 Supercharger cabinet in Redwood City, California, offering 500 kW capacity, higher power density, and increased stall efficiency. Tesla announced that owners in Australia and New Zealand have driven over 621,371 miles (1 million kilometers) using Supervised FSD within two weeks of its launch in the region. The company also noted it is conducting FSD testing in Japan. Additionally, CEO Elon Musk hinted at a potential update for the Summon function to navigate multi-level parking garages, and noted that Tesla must reach over 10 million active FSD subscriptions to fulfill specific compensation milestones.

Tesla ranked as the second most-searched ticker on Benzinga Pro for September 2025, maintaining its position among the top three most-searched stocks throughout the third quarter. Tesla also faces challenges related to CEO Elon Musk's political involvement.

Tesla increased lease prices for all US vehicles following the September 30 expiration of the $7,500 federal tax credit. The Model Y monthly lease price rose to $529–$599, while the Model 3 increased to $429–$759. Purchase prices remain unchanged. The loss of incentives is expected to impact demand amid existing sales pressure and competition.

Farley emphasized the need for supply chain flexibility and innovation to compete with Chinese automakers like BYD, which he described as a 700-pound gorilla in the EV industry. He noted that he is less concerned about competition from Tesla, GM, or Ford compared to Chinese manufacturers.

US Senators Ed Markey and Richard Blumenthal have urged the NHTSA to investigate Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, citing safety concerns at railroad crossings. The NHTSA is collecting information on the incidents. In October 2024, the agency opened an investigation into 2.4 million Tesla vehicles regarding FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Tesla recently clarified on its website that the FSD package does not render vehicles autonomous.

Eightco Holdings Chairman Dan Ives announced plans to acquire 800 million World Network (WLD) tokens, currently valued at $992 million, as part of the 'Power of 8' program. Tesla reported third-quarter statistics, including 4,000 new Supercharger stations (+18% YoY), 1.8 TWh of charging energy delivered (+29% YoY), and 842 million liters of gasoline saved. The Tesla Diner in California served 50,000 burgers over 72 days. Additionally, Tesla announced record quarterly deliveries of 497,099 units.

Ford, GM, and Tesla reported record EV deliveries in Q3, partly driven by the expiration of the $7,500 federal tax credit on September 30. Ford's Q3 EV sales reached 85,789, up 19.8% year-over-year, while GM sold a record 66,501 EVs. Tesla delivered 497,009 vehicles. Automakers are concerned about a potential demand pullback in Q1 2026 without the tax credit and are exploring ways to maintain sales momentum.

This deal triggered buying across the AI sector, including NVIDIA, Vertiv, Dell, Super Micro, and Western Digital. Early money flows for Tesla (TSLA) were positive.

Tesla reported record Q3 2025 results with 497,099 vehicle deliveries and 12.5 GWh of energy storage deployments. Model 3/Y accounted for 481,166 deliveries and 435,826 units produced. Elon Musk has become the first person to reach a $500 billion net worth, a figure exceeding the GDP of 83% of the world's nations. His fortune is primarily driven by his 15.3% stake in Tesla, which accounts for nearly 47% of his wealth. Tesla stock rose 37.6% from August to $459.46, increasing his holdings by $63.9 billion in just over a month.

Tesla stock rose 1.86% in pre-market trading following Elon Musk's milestone of reaching a $500 billion net worth. Musk's wealth is supported by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. Tesla is currently preparing for Q3 delivery reports, with investor Gary Black anticipating strong results. While the Cybertruck has shown performance capabilities, the company faces challenges including declining European sales and potential demand concerns once the $7,500 EV credit expires.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang praised Elon Musk's cognitive abilities, calling him the 'ultimate GPU' for his capacity to manage complex interdependencies across his companies. Musk is currently developing 'Colossus II,' a gigawatt AI training cluster utilizing at least 200,000 Nvidia GPUs. Nvidia stock is up 35.38% year-to-date, while Tesla is up 21.14%.

Tesla released a video showing a Cybertruck beating a Porsche 911 in a drag race while towing another 911. Despite this, the Cybertruck faces slow U.S. sales, with over 46,000 units sold in 14 months against a 250,000 annual production capacity. Tesla has discontinued the $69,990 RWD Long Range variant and currently holds over 10,000 unsold units in the U.S.

Tesla's sales in Italy fell over 25.6% in September, with year-to-date sales in the region down 32.36% compared to 2024. Despite European struggles, Tesla's Berlin Gigafactory is increasing production. Tesla is expected to beat Q3 delivery consensus due to U.S. demand ahead of the September 30 EV credit deadline, while Chinese sales remain steady with 165,350 units sold in September.

The Federal EV tax credit expired on October 1, 2025. Tesla previously generated $2.8 billion in 2024 revenue from selling ZEV credits, a revenue stream now threatened by the Trump administration's relaxation of CAFE norms.

Elon Musk reached a $500 billion net worth, fueled by Tesla's stock rally, a $400 billion valuation for SpaceX, and a $113 billion valuation for xAI. Tesla shares are up 21.14% year-to-date. Musk owns 12% of Tesla, valued at $191 billion. While a 2018 compensation package was voided, the board has proposed a new plan potentially worth up to $1 trillion, which has faced criticism from figures including Bernie Sanders and Pope Leo XIV.

Tesla shares rose 3.31% to $459.46, trading within a 52-week range of $212.11 to $488.54. The increase follows the expiration of the $7,500 federal EV tax credit, prompting Tesla to adjust its U.S. lease pricing across all models.

Tesla's proposed pay package for CEO Elon Musk is entirely milestone-based, requiring him to stay for at least 7.5 years and hit targets including market capitalization (up to $8.5 trillion), adjusted EBITDA, vehicle deliveries, FSD subscribers, robotaxis, and humanoid robot deliveries. Market expert Jay Woods compares this to an NFL player contract without a salary, relying solely on performance incentives. The package has faced criticism from figures like Thomas DiNapoli and Bernie Sanders as excessive, while Tesla's board supports it, citing strong historical stock returns.

His return coincided with announcements regarding robotaxis and autonomy, contributing to a 22% stock surge in May.

Tesla announced a new lease program for Powerwall and solar connections in the U.S., featuring low monthly payments and maintenance coverage, with an option to buy out the service after five years. Former Tesla President Jon McNeill stated the EV market can grow without the $7,500 Federal EV Credit, which ended September 30. Tesla applied for a patent for an 'inflatable aerodynamic deflector' for the Cybertruck to reduce drag and improve efficiency. Tesla discontinued the $69,990 RWD Long Range trim and increased the Cyberbeast price to $114,990. The company currently holds an inventory of approximately 10,000 unsold Cybertrucks in the U.S.

Tesla and CEO Elon Musk are defending a new trillion-dollar pay package and Musk's $500 billion fortune. Musk stated the vote is about retaining control of the company. The package has faced criticism from figures including Sen. Bernie Sanders and Pope Leo. Tesla has launched an ad campaign urging shareholders to vote in favor of the board's recommendations.

Tesla shares declined 5.11% to close at $436 on Thursday, following reports of a 25.6% drop in September car sales in Italy. Benzinga's Edge Stock Rankings place Tesla in the 4th percentile for value.

The parents of 19-year-old Krysta Tsukahara have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against Tesla following a fatal Cybertruck accident in November in Piedmont, California. The suit alleges that the vehicle's electronic door system, which relies on a 12-volt battery, malfunctioned after a collision with a tree, trapping the victim inside as the vehicle caught fire. The lawsuit claims manual door releases are difficult to locate in emergencies and seeks unspecified punitive damages.

Elon Musk maintains that failure is necessary for innovation as long as it is not catastrophic, comparing the process to a baseball batting average rather than individual at-bats. This philosophy, which he articulated during a 2020 symposium, guides the iterative design processes at both SpaceX and Tesla. Musk views stagnation as a greater threat than non-fatal setbacks and encourages teams to push boundaries even if it leads to failures.

Tesla was a top retail focus from September 29 to October 3, 2025, amid the expiration of the Federal EV Credit on October 1 and the announcement of record Q3 deliveries and energy deployments. Despite this, the stock fell on Thursday following reports of a 25.6% decline in Italian car sales. As of the report, Tesla traded around $436 to $442 per share, with a 52-week range of $212.11 to $488.54, up 14.95% year-to-date.

Tesla and other major automakers including Ford, GM, Toyota, and Honda may benefit from potential tariff relief being considered by President Donald Trump. Sen. Bernie Moreno indicated that the administration might extend and expand a 3.75% import adjustment offset for U.S.-assembled vehicles to five years and include engine production. While Ford CEO Jim Farley warned that existing 25% tariffs on auto parts and vehicles could create a $2 billion headwind, other executives have supported the broader tariff strategy to encourage domestic job creation.

Following the September 30 expiration of the federal EV tax credit, Ford, GM, Stellantis, Hyundai, and Lucid are extending incentives to customers. Tesla, Rivian, Honda, and Toyota are not offering extensions. Tesla had previously urged customers to lock in purchases by September 30. Former Tesla sales chief Jon McNeill believes EV sales will grow without incentives due to model availability, while Gene Munster of Deepwater Asset Management suggests the end of credits benefits Tesla by reducing traditional automakers' incentive to invest in EVs.

President Donald Trump's recent policies are impacting several sectors. Approximately 100,000 federal employees are expected to resign as part of a new administration program. In the automotive sector, Tesla and other manufacturers are bracing for a potential market disruption following the expiration of federal EV subsidies. Additionally, the administration is integrating nuclear energy into national security and AI infrastructure, and USA Rare Earth is in talks with the White House.

A group of Tesla shareholders, including SOC Investment Group and various state officials, is urging a vote against Elon Musk's $1 trillion pay package at the upcoming November meeting, citing governance concerns and the expiration of EV tax credits. The board is also facing opposition regarding the re-election of directors Ira Ehrenpreis, Joe Gebbia, and Kathleen Wilson-Thompson. Morgan Stanley analyst Adam Jonas supports the pay package, while Tesla maintains it is tied to performance and shareholder value creation.

Tesla and Amazon, which account for 42% of the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY), are underperforming in 2025, with Amazon's stock flat and Tesla up 6%. This concentration has caused the XLY to trail the S&P 500 by 12% year-to-date. In contrast, the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Consumer Discretionary ETF (RSPD) is up 9.2% year-to-date, suggesting that smaller and mid-sized companies in the sector are performing well.

The UK has become BYD's largest international market, with the company selling 11,271 vehicles there in September 2025, compared to 1,150 in September 2024. BYD's UK market share is 3.6%, second to Tesla. Meanwhile, Tesla's sales in the UK fell to 987 units in July 2025 from 2,462 in July 2024, and its Italian sales dropped over 25% in September. BYD also cut its annual production forecast by over 16% to 4.6 million units.

Tesla released cryptic teasers on X, including videos of headlights and a spinning turbine with a '10/7' date, sparking speculation about an affordable Model Y, a new GPU, or the Roadster. A patent granted on August 5, 2025, for an adaptive aerodynamics system involving fans has fueled theories regarding the Roadster. YouTuber Marques Brownlee recently canceled his $50,000 booking for the Roadster.

Elon Musk shared a video of a Tesla Optimus robot performing martial arts, confirming it is operating autonomously rather than being teleoperated. Tesla reported Q3 2025 deliveries of 497,099 units, beating estimates. Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives raised his Tesla price target to $600, citing the company's AI and robotics push, though Ashish Kumar, head of Optimus AI, is reportedly leaving for Meta.

Eric Jackson, a supporter of Opendoor Technologies, believes the company can capture 10% of U.S. housing transactions, drawing a comparison to Tesla's goal of reaching 10% of global auto sales. Jackson notes that this outlook depends on Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, as current 30-year mortgage rates of 6% to 7% are unsustainable.

Plug Power's stock has risen over 160% in the last month, boosting clean-energy ETFs like the Global X Hydrogen ETF (HYDR) and ALPS Clean Energy ETF (ACES). HYDR, a concentrated hydrogen fund, is up 71% year-to-date. ACES, which is more diversified and includes Tesla as a top 10 holding, is up 21% year-to-date. Plug Power also announced a CEO succession plan, with José Luis Crespo becoming president on Oct. 10 and CEO in early 2026.

Tesla stock fell 3.97% to $435.24 on Tuesday amid concerns over profit margins and potential sales cannibalization following the launch of the Model Y Standard ($39,990) and Model 3 Standard. While the stock remains above its 50-day moving average of $366.40, it faces resistance at $452.68 and support at $334.41. Benzinga Edge rankings show a momentum score of 90.15 but a weak value score of 4.21.

Tesla unveiled a new Model Y priced at $39,990 on Tuesday, alongside updates to its supervised self-driving technology. The announcement followed a period of online speculation fueled by cryptic teasers. Tesla stock is up 9% in 2025, having rebounded 40% in the third quarter after a 36% decline in the first quarter.

A neutral call option sweep for TSLA was recorded on October 10, 2025, with a $450.00 strike price. The trade involved 26 contracts split into 7 trades, totaling $26.9K in cost, with 76,164 contracts traded today against 20,809 existing open contracts.

Bank of America Securities analyst Vivek Arya stated that fears regarding vendor financing in the semiconductor industry are overstated, noting that such arrangements account for only 5–10% of projected AI capital expenditures. Arya highlighted that Tesla's xAI initiative involves little to no vendor financing, contrasting it with Nvidia's and AMD's commitments.

The Arora Report noted negative money flows for Tesla (TSLA) in early trading. The report mentioned an upcoming Tesla event with expectations for a lower-cost Model Y and rumors regarding potential entry into the eVOL business, which have influenced buying in Joby Aviation and Archer Aviation.

Rivian expects R2 deliveries by August 2026, while Tesla is teasing an October 7 event.

Ross Gerber criticized speculation regarding a potential affordable Tesla Model Y trim, calling it 'really sad' on X. Gary Black also expressed concern that a cheaper Model Y would not increase brand leverage or expand the total addressable market. Tesla delivered over 497,000 units in Q3, exceeding analyst estimates, but Gerber warned of a difficult Q4 ahead.

Gary Black, managing director of Future Fund LLC, argued that Tesla's brand leverage is underdeveloped and that a new affordable Model Y variant might cannibalize existing sales rather than drive incremental volume. Black, who has advocated for a cheaper form factor since 2020, cited Apple's iPhone 16e as a successful example of using lower-priced products to broaden brand appeal. Gene Munster also expressed concerns regarding potential cannibalization.

Tesla showcased custom Optimus humanoid robots at the premiere of Disney's 'Tron: ARES' starring Jared Leto. The event featured interactive AI-generated avatars and a video of Leto interacting with a robot. CEO Elon Musk has stated Optimus could represent over 80% of Tesla's future value, though the company recently saw the departure of its head of Optimus AI, Ashish Kumar. Tesla is scheduled to unveil a new product on October 7.

Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney maintained a Neutral rating on Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) with a $425 price target, noting that new Standard variants of the Model 3 ($36,990) and Model Y ($39,990) are less unique in price and features than expected. Delaney suggested the China-launched Model Y L may be more significant for growth and identified risks including competition, potential price cuts, and FSD delays. Tesla stock is up 14.2% year-to-date in 2025.

Goldman Sachs strategist Peter Oppenheimer argues that the current AI-driven tech boom is fundamentally healthier than the 2000 dot-com bubble. The Magnificent Seven, including Tesla, trade at a median forward P/E of 26.8, significantly lower than the 52x median seen in 2000. Unlike past bubbles, current leaders possess strong balance sheets and free cash flows, though Goldman warns that earnings disappointment remains a risk.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained an Outperform rating and $600 price target for Tesla, viewing affordable models as a step toward a 500k quarterly delivery run-rate, despite expressing disappointment that the price reduction is only $5k. Ives projects a $2 trillion market cap by early 2026, driven by FSD V14.1 improvements and robotics. Other analysts, including Gary Black and Ross Gerber, have criticized the new affordable models for lacking features and potential market appeal.

Ross Gerber and Gary Black criticized Tesla's new affordable Model Y and Model 3 trims. Gerber warned that the $7,500 tax credit is being "pilfered" and suggested that buyers will lose money, advising them to lease instead. Gary Black argued the trims lack features like exterior light bars, 2nd-row touch screens, and premium sound systems, stating he does not see them selling despite the sub-$40k price tag.

Nvidia is reportedly investing up to $2 billion in Elon Musk's xAI as part of a $20 billion funding round. The funds, which include equity and debt, will be used to purchase Nvidia GPUs for xAI's 'Colossus 2' AI data center in Memphis, Tennessee. Tesla shareholders are scheduled to vote later this year on whether the automaker should invest directly in xAI.

Martin Shkreli defended Tesla and Elon Musk against short sellers, stating that Musk has already succeeded. Tesla stock is up 14.8% year-to-date in 2025, trading between $212.11 and $488.56. Approximately 2.96% of the Tesla float, or 86.21 million shares valued at over $37 billion, is currently shorted. Musk has previously warned that short sellers will be "obliterated" if they do not exit positions before Tesla achieves autonomy at scale.

A whale alert for TSLA options on October 9, 2025, identified a bearish put option sweep. Traders purchased 55 contracts at a $430.00 strike price, expiring on October 10, 2025. The total trade price was $33.5K, with 87.8K volume and 18.5K open interest at that strike.

Tesla is identified as a member of the "Magnificent Seven," a group of companies that reached a record $21 trillion market cap in September. Direxion offers two ETFs for speculating on these stocks: the Direxion Daily Magnificent 7 Bull 2X Shares (QQQU), which has gained 28% year-to-date, and the Direxion Daily Magnificent 7 Bear 1X Shares (QQQD), which has seen a 20% year-to-date loss.

Tesla shares fell 2.14% to $429.52 following reports that the NHTSA is opening an investigation into 2.88 million Tesla vehicles equipped with Full Self-Driving (FSD). The probe follows 58 reports of safety violations, including 14 crashes and 23 injuries, with incidents involving vehicles driving through red lights and in the wrong direction. Tesla recently released an FSD software update, and CEO Elon Musk stated on X that vehicles will feel 'sentient' by version 14.3.

China is tightening export controls on rare earth minerals, with new licensing requirements for military and high-tech applications taking effect immediately or on December 1. Additionally, China will require licenses for lithium battery exports starting November 8. TSMC reported a 31.4% year-over-year revenue increase for September, boosting the AI trade. Money flows are currently negative for Tesla (TSLA) and positive for Nvidia (NVDA).

Tesla CEO Elon Musk could potentially earn billions from a new pay package even if specific milestones are not met. Reuters reported Musk could net over $26 billion by hitting two targets and maintaining stock growth, with a maximum of $93 billion possible if all goals—including 1 million vehicle deliveries, 1 million Optimus robots, 10 million FSD subscriptions, and 1 million Robotaxis—are met at a $3.5 trillion valuation. Tesla recently debuted the Optimus robot and introduced affordable Model Y and Model 3 trims, which received mixed reactions from analysts like Dan Ives.

General Motors unveiled the Chevrolet Bolt EV with a $29,000 starting price, featuring a 255-mile range and a 65 kWh LFP battery from CATL. The base LT trim is priced at $28,995, with higher trims reaching $32,000. GM is backtracking on planned EV incentives following concerns from Sen. Bernie Moreno. Meanwhile, Tesla's new affordable Model Y and Model 3 trims have faced criticism from experts like Ross Gerber and Gary Black, who doubt they will significantly increase sales.

YouTuber Zack Nelson (JerryRigEverything) criticized Rivian for its repair policy, noting the company charged $775 for a 12v battery replacement and warned that third-party repairs could void warranties. Nelson praised Tesla's repair policies, stating the company provides manuals for owners. Separately, Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe confirmed R2 crossover deliveries are expected around August 2026, while Lucid Group reported record Q3 2025 production and delivery figures.

China has tightened export controls on rare earth minerals and related technologies, citing national security concerns. The new rules require approval for exporting magnets containing trace amounts of Chinese-sourced materials or produced using Chinese technology. This move impacts tech companies including Nvidia, Apple, and Tesla. The U.S. is seeking supply chain alternatives, such as a $500 million partnership with Pakistan, and has equity stakes in miners like MP Materials Corp.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives predicts a strong Q3 earnings season for tech, driven by robust AI demand. LPL Financial reports that tariff impacts on Q3 earnings are less severe than anticipated. The Magnificent 7, excluding Tesla, are expected to drive 70% of the S&P 500's projected 8% earnings growth. Tesla's YTD performance is 15.66%.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk shared a video of the Optimus robot performing Kung Fu autonomously, without human teleoperation. Jim Cramer credited Nvidia's AI technology for this achievement. Nvidia is a key supplier for Tesla's AI systems, with Musk's Colossus II supercomputer project utilizing over 200,000 Nvidia GPUs. Nvidia is also reportedly investing up to $2 billion in Musk's xAI venture.

General Motors has canceled plans to offer EV incentives beyond the September 30 Federal EV Credit deadline following concerns raised by Sen. Bernie Moreno. Ford and Stellantis continue to offer incentives, while Hyundai has slashed prices on the Ioniq 5. Jon McNeill, a GM board member and former Tesla sales chief, stated that U.S. EV sales will grow despite the rollback of incentives.

Gerber also warned against buying cheaper Tesla models, recommending leasing instead. Institutional investors like CalPERS, ISS, and Glass Lewis oppose the deal, while the board argues it is necessary to maintain Musk's focus.

Additionally, SpaceX and xAI are purchasing Cybertrucks to replace their support fleets as Tesla manages rising inventory and lower-than-expected demand for the pickup.

Tesla reported 26,000 vehicle sales in China for October 2025, a 36% year-over-year decline and the company's worst monthly performance in three years. Year-to-date deliveries in China are down by approximately 40,000 units. Tesla also faces declining registrations in Europe, down over 30% year-to-date. Increased competition from lower-priced Chinese automakers and the expiration of U.S. federal EV tax credits on Sept. 30 are contributing to demand challenges.

XPeng unveiled its humanoid robot 'Iron' at its AI Day, with plans for mass production by the end of 2026. Unlike Tesla's Optimus, which is envisioned for complex tasks, XPeng intends to use Iron for roles like museum guides and receptionists. XPeng is also developing a 'Land Aircraft Carrier' hybrid van for 2026 production, while Tesla prepares to roll out Cybercab robotaxis in the U.S. next year.

Tesla shares fell following a report of 26,006 vehicle sales in China for October, a 35.8% drop from October 2024. Tesla's market share in China fell to 3.2%, while competitor Xiaomi reported record sales of 48,654 units. The company also faces weak sales in major European markets. Additionally, Cathie Wood's Ark Invest sold 5,426 Tesla shares for approximately $2.4 million.

CoreWeave (CRWV) stock fell below $100 following earnings where it reported adjusted EPS of $0.22 versus a $0.40 consensus and revenue of $1.36B against a $1.29B consensus. Revenue projections were lowered to $5.05B–$5.15B. SoftBank has sold its Nvidia (NVDA) and some T-Mobile (TMUS) shares to fund a $30B investment in OpenAI. Money flows are currently negative for several Magnificent Seven stocks, including Tesla (TSLA), Nvidia, Microsoft, Meta, Alphabet, and Amazon.

A SIFMA report indicates that the top 20 companies in the S&P 500 account for 50.8% of the index's total market value as of October 31, 2025, despite generating only 9.1% of total earnings. The Magnificent 7, including Tesla (TSLA), Nvidia, and Apple, hold a record 35.3% index weight. Tesla carries a forward P/E ratio of 328.3, while the median forward P/E for the top 20 stocks is 36.6, significantly higher than the historical median of 25.4.

Hesai Technology (HSAI) has been named the exclusive lidar supplier for Li Auto's next-generation assisted driving platform, covering the 'L' Series, 'i' Series, and 'MEGA' models. Li Auto has delivered over 1.46 million vehicles as of October 31, 2025. Hesai reported Q3 2025 adjusted EPS of 28 cents and revenue of $111.7 million, with 441,398 lidar units shipped.

Tesla Inc.'s (TSLA) short interest has decreased by 5.3% since the last report, with 72.69 million shares sold short, representing 2.5% of the float. It would take approximately 1.0 days to cover these positions. Tesla's short interest as a percentage of float is lower than its peer group average of 8.59%.

Tech pioneer Andrew Ng advocates for AI-assisted 'vibe coding' to lower barriers to software development. The article notes that former Tesla AI director Andrej Karpathy believes artificial general intelligence is still years away and warns that large language models remain unreliable. Additionally, Nvidia unveiled the Rubin CPX GPU, which offers 7.5 times more performance than previous systems.

Elon Musk and Donald Trump have proposed different strategies to manage the $38 trillion U.S. national debt. Musk advocates for technology-fueled growth, specifically through AI and robotics, as the "only way" to address the deficit. Conversely, Trump plans to use "massive Tariff Income" to pay down the debt after providing $2,000 payments to citizens. Financial expert Ray Dalio warned of a potential "debt death spiral" involving currency devaluation and inflation.

Elon Musk addressed criticism regarding his $1 trillion pay package and Tesla's valuation, noting the irony that critics often claim Tesla is overvalued while simultaneously arguing that his performance-based stock award is excessive. The pay package, which could make Musk the first trillionaire, was supported by over 75% of shareholders. Musk acknowledged the targets are a "tall order" but expressed confidence in achieving them through immense work.

Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, sold 5,426 Tesla shares for approximately $2.4 million across its ARKK and ARKW ETFs. Concurrently, Ark purchased $2.51 million in Pony AI shares, $4.9 million in TSMC shares, and $12.4 million in Baidu shares. The trades reflect a strategic focus on AI and tech, while Tesla continues to shift toward self-reliance in semiconductor production to enhance its AI capabilities.

Elon Musk stated that his companies, including Tesla and his AI startup xAI, are "trending towards convergence." This follows a recent shareholder meeting where a non-binding proposal for Tesla to invest in xAI did not pass. Morgan Stanley analysts suggested that investors may need to revisit the importance of xAI to Tesla, as the two companies are already collaborating on AI models like Grok.

The U.S. Supreme Court is reviewing a case regarding tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). If the court strikes down these tariffs, import-heavy companies like Tesla, Apple, Amazon, and various retailers could see improved margins and earnings due to reduced supply chain costs. Conversely, domestic manufacturers like Nucor and Alcoa, which benefited from tariff-related protection, may face increased competitive pressure.

Benzinga's options activity scanner identified a neutral call option trade for TSLA expiring on November 14, 2025. The trade involved 40 contracts at a $430.00 strike price, with a total trade price of $29.4K and a volume of 30.7K.
In early trading, money flows are positive for Tesla Inc. (TSLA) alongside other Magnificent Seven stocks like Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, and Microsoft.

STMicroelectronics, a supplier to Tesla and Apple, saw its stock rise after CEO Jean-Marc Chery projected a return to normal levels in 2026. The company previously reported a 460 bps decline in gross margin to 33.2% and an operating margin drop to 5.6% in its third-quarter 2025 results, leading to a 2025 capex cut below $2 billion.

Future Fund LLC's Gary Black criticized Tesla's pivot to autonomy and robots as a 'fool's narrative,' noting that over 70% of profits come from EVs. Tesla's October global volumes dropped 30-35% year-over-year, with China sales falling 35.8% to 26,006 units and European registrations declining 36.3%. Analyst Chris Pierce of Needham warned that half of Tesla's projected 2030-2035 gross profit relies on unproven businesses. Tesla shares closed at $439.62 on Tuesday, down 1.26%.

In his final annual letter as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, Warren Buffett criticized escalating CEO compensation, referencing Elon Musk's revised pay deal as part of a cycle driven by 'envy and greed.' Musk has defended his compensation package, which is tied to Tesla's future valuation.

Journalist Andrew Sorkin discussed his 2023 DealBook Summit interview with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, stating he aimed to show Musk's 'true self' and different versions of his personality. Musk also recently touted X's AI-powered ad targeting using xAI's Grok. Additionally, SpaceX's Starlink launched a $40/month unlimited data plan in select U.S. areas.

The Trump administration is considering an executive order to limit the influence of proxy advisors like Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) and Glass Lewis, potentially banning shareholder recommendations. This follows Tesla CEO Elon Musk's public criticism of these firms for opposing his recently approved trillion-dollar pay package.

Ford CEO Jim Farley stated he was 'humbled' by Tesla's technology after his team disassembled a Model 3 and found it used significantly less electrical wiring than the Mustang Mach-E. Despite production challenges and potential shutdowns for the F-150 Lightning, Farley reaffirmed Ford's commitment to EVs, predicting U.S. EV adoption will be near 5%.

SpaceX introduced a new 'Residential 100 MBPS' Starlink plan in the U.S. for $40/month, featuring unlimited data and free installation. Elon Musk praised the deal, which is available in select areas. Musk also mentioned that SpaceX could potentially become a public company in the future.

An investment of $1,000 in Tesla (TSLA) 15 years ago would be worth $201,734.69 today, based on a current price of $402.25. Tesla has achieved an average annual return of 42.1% over the past 15 years and currently holds a market capitalization of $1.34 trillion.

Tech stocks lost over $700 billion in market value on Thursday due to fading Fed rate-cut hopes and concerns over AI-related power infrastructure bottlenecks. Tesla shares fell 6.7%, contributing to a $100 billion combined market cap loss with Alphabet. Federal Reserve officials signaled a 'high bar' for rate cuts, dampening market sentiment.

Tradr ETFs launched four new 2x leveraged single-stock ETFs tracking Bloom Energy, Celestica, NANO Nuclear Energy, and Synopsys, all linked to AI infrastructure. The firm, which manages over $2 billion in assets, previously established leveraged products tracking Tesla and Nvidia.

U.S. markets fell on Thursday as investors repriced Fed rate-cut expectations following hawkish comments from central bank officials. The Nasdaq 100 dropped 1.7%, and Tesla shares fell 6.32%. Market-implied odds for a December rate cut fell below 50%.

Benzinga's options activity scanner identified a bullish call option sweep for TSLA expiring on November 14, 2025. The trade involved 449 contracts at a $410.00 strike price, split across 63 trades.
